name: Phantom & Crimson Solitaire
line:
Beneath the darkening sky, you find yourself lost in a fog-shrouded forest.
You are in desperate need of safe shelter to pass the night,
and it appears that the ominous castle ahead is your only choice.

zones.name: Mist-stricken Missteps
zones.desc: There is fog covering the entire area, and our squad has lost our way. Are we pushing forward, or are we merely dissolving ourselves away?

zones.name: Sunset Courtyard
zones.desc: The courtyard is filled with the sunset's afterglow, yet that is not the color of hope. Come in, come in, esteemed guests who have wandered astray. The castle welcomes you.

zones.name: Homeland Remnants
zones.desc: As the mist spreads into the castle and the vines make their way up the columns, the dark night appears to have retreated elsewhere for the moment. Do remember as you enjoy this moment of respite, however, that darkness is always right around the corner.

zones.name: Nightmare's Prelude
zones.desc: Darkness falls, the candles throughout the deserted castle begin to light up by themselves, and the silent Nightzmora begins to roam the shadows in search for prey who have lost their way.

zones.name: Blood-soaked Corridor
zones.desc: Blood moons often signify the beginning of unfortunate tragedies in written accounts, and this omen is now shining down upon us through the windowpanes that line the corridor.

zones.name: Hall of Desires
zones.desc: The purest and truest desires, standing above all time, have engulfed this space whole. Here, there is nothing but the endless thirst for aesthetics, fine arts, and perfection.

nodeTypes.name: Combat Ops.
nodeTypes.desc: Everyone has their weapons drawn. They're definitely not here to negotiate.
nodeTypes.func: A standard combat node. Win to earn collectibles, Originium Ingots, and other rewards.

nodeTypes.name: Emergency Ops.
nodeTypes.desc: Some violence may well exceed your expectations, a very common occurrence.
nodeTypes.func: A harder fight with stronger enemies than regular Combat Ops — and better rewards (Hope, etc.) to match.

nodeTypes.name: Dreadful Foe
nodeTypes.desc: Criminals, ruffians, and the damned… You're in trouble.
nodeTypes.func: The boss battle guarding the end of each floor. You must clear it to move on to the next floor.

nodeTypes.name: Rogue Trader
nodeTypes.desc: Not an ally, or an enemy. Some people like to sit on the fence.
nodeTypes.func: A shop where you spend Originium Ingots on collectibles and items, or restore Objective HP.

nodeTypes.name: Safe House
nodeTypes.desc: Quiet, comfortable, and secluded from the castle's trifling matters. It's time to take a break.
nodeTypes.func: A rest node offering upkeep options such as restoring Objective HP or temporarily promoting Operators.

nodeTypes.name: Encounter
nodeTypes.desc: New friends, old rivals… Strange encounters never age. Only scars stand the test of time.
nodeTypes.func: A random-event node. Depending on your choices you gain rewards — or pay a price.

nodeTypes.name: Boons
nodeTypes.desc: A little bit of help for your journey.
nodeTypes.func: A treasure node that grants collectibles and other rewards without a fight.

nodeTypes.name: Downtime Recreation
nodeTypes.desc: Give away all your resources in the name of art.
nodeTypes.func: A recreation node with theme-specific minigames and wagers. Rewards depend on the outcome.

nodeTypes.name: Dense Fog
nodeTypes.desc: There's something in the mist, but you cannot see anything...
nodeTypes.func: An unidentified node hidden from view. Its true type is revealed when you enter.

difficulties.name: Castle Sightseeing
difficulties.rule: 'Silent Squad' will escort you the whole way

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: Threats lurking in the castle should not be taken lightly

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: Hallucinations will appear inside the castle

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: Start with a Playwright's Manuscript and a regular Collectible at random

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: Start with a negative Collectible and a regular Collectible at random

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: All Elite and Leader enemies gain +20% HP

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: Crimson Troupe Plays will be performed in the castle

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: Upon entering 'Homeland Remnants', obtain a random Playwright's Manuscript

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: New Hallucinations will appear in the castle, and increased chance of having 2 Hallucinations simultaneously

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: After reaching 'Nightmare's Prelude', all 6-star Operators cost +1 Hope to recruit

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: Enemies in Emergency Ops and Dreadful Foe nodes gain +15% ATK and HP; Events leading to a different ending have a greatly increased chance of appearing

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: Specific Hallucination pairs will appear, and combo effects will trigger

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: Start with a reforged negative Collectible and a regular Collectible at random

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: For the first 20 seconds after they appear, Elite and Leader enemies take 50% less Physical and Arts damage and have +30% ATK

difficulties.name: Formal Investigation
difficulties.rule: Upon reaching 'Blood-soaked Corridor', obtain a Negative Collectible and a special regular Collectible at random

difficulties.name: Monthly Squad
difficulties.rule: Squads are refreshed on the first day of each month

difficulties.name: Deep Investigation
difficulties.rule: A challenge mode that provides a unique experience.

stages.name: A Date With Slugs
stages.desc: In a play where the ending is yet to be decided, man does not necessarily have more of a presence than Originium Slugs.

stages.name: Beast Taming
stages.desc:
The moment the two beast tamers blow their whistles, the hounds all scramble out of their enclosure to feast. 
 Periodically emits Pulse Waves to the surrounding tiles, damaging both allied and enemy units.

stages.name: Gun Salute
stages.desc: When there are visitors, we have to welcome them with a gun salute. With the fog covering the entire area, though, we might have a little trouble with the firing trajectory.

stages.name: Accident
stages.desc: The actors are practicing how to avoid landing on traps as they parachute down, yet your arrival has kicked their training intensity up a notch.

stages.name: Fight to the Death
stages.desc:
A dance in fire; a competition for the dancer's attention. 
There are  that will periodically deal damage to units above them.

stages.name: The Grand Finale
stages.desc: Don't just worry about dealing with the mercenaries and Originium slugs. The most troublesome folks always show up last.

stages.name: Patrol Squad
stages.desc: A team of mercenaries are patrolling in the courtyard. Their hounds pick up the scent of strangers and start barking immediately, sending the entire squad your way.

stages.name: Destitute Knights
stages.desc: A ground of expelled Kazimierzian knights have become wandering mercenaries, their former titles revealed by the insignias on their clothing.

stages.name: Unequal Split
stages.desc: The bandits and casters are having an argument over the unowned treasure. Just as they are about to come to blows, your entrance gives them a different idea.

stages.name: First Come, Last Served
stages.desc: You encounter a band of mercenaries who have also lost their way. Short on food, they set their sights on your bag.

stages.name: Justice
stages.desc:
An Ursus tragedy is unfolding yet again here in the castle. The choice you make is no different from that of the countless of men who came before you.
DP recovery rate is decreased, and tiles do not have melee/ranged restrictions

stages.name: A Familiar Face
stages.desc:
A Sarkaz woman and her underling... Though you do not remember the details, you are certain you have gone through this before.
 Operators deployed on this tile will restore HP over time.

stages.name: Vintage Transport
stages.desc: You see a pile of goods scattered across the ground and a swarm of Originium slugs rampaging about. The flustered transport team's manager is trying to catch all the escaping slugs. Perhaps you can help him.

stages.name: With The Crowd
stages.desc: There's no telling who it was that yelled, but a bustling crowd starts to swarm toward you.

stages.name: Beast Fighting
stages.desc:
This place is originally meant for watching hounds fight each other, but now...
Operators will lose a certain percentage of HP when they are initially deployed

stages.name: Premiere
stages.desc: This is the first time some members of the audience are watching a performance of the troupe. The dancers' alluring postures have made many of them impetuous and easily angered. They will not tolerate even a single word of criticism against the dancers.

stages.name: Reform
stages.desc: The troupe accepted these prisoners and promised them freedom. What these convicts don't know is that even though their bodies will be freed from this moment, their souls will forever be imprisoned.

stages.name: Sculptor and Statue
stages.desc:
The sculptor needs his livelihood. The statue needs maintenance. Who will provide the resources? Only the passing traveler. 
There are  that can be knocked down by neighboring Operators, dealing damage to the two tiles it falls on and forming rubble that blocks routes; No melee/ranged deployment restrictions

stages.name: Pressing Ahead
stages.desc:
The enemies of the past all emerge from the shadows one by one, yet their equipment seems multitudes better than before.
DP recovery rate is decreased, and tiles do not have melee/ranged restrictions

stages.name: Shrouded in Clouds
stages.desc:
With how difficult it is to breathe here because of all the smog, you want to get away as soon as possible. The enemies lurking in the dark have a different idea, though.
 Operators lose HP constantly.

stages.name: Fireworks Show
stages.desc: The actors setting the stage are ready to go. All there is left to do is to have the audience members come forward and 'place' the fireworks on their heads.

stages.name: Unending
stages.desc:
A group of Sarkaz men are having nightmares. Driven by their fear, they scramble ever forward,
 Pay a cost to activate it, damaging and stunning nearby enemies.

stages.name: Traveler From Afar
stages.desc: These men are here in search of allies who will help the Taran cause, no matter if the 'artists' here are good or evil.

stages.name: A Dance Together
stages.desc: The dancers and audience members are dancing together, and there are balloons sending presents to everyone all around the ballroom. The night is young, and the excitement has just begun.

stages.name: Bob's Beers
stages.desc: You see a group of ruffians sorting out the supplies they made off with—a large amount of Originium slug beer and drones transporting infused Originium slug extract. Perhaps you can help the brewers get their supplies back. They'll be very happy about it.

stages.name: Alpine Visitor
stages.desc:
Not everyone who has seen the world outside is willing to return to the land of ice and snow. Some have chosen to abandon Karlan for a new life. 
There are  that periodically inflict [Cold] to nearby units.

stages.name: Drone Landing Zone
stages.desc: The Troupe isn't a conservative, ancient organization. In addition to their pursuit in their classical interests, they also take pleasure in employing modern technologies, such as the swarm of drones above your head.

stages.name: The Red Mist
stages.desc:
It is now midnight, and the ominous red mist is still spreading throughout the area. The mist is seeking out souls that still have sanity left in order to drag them into the deep abyss.
 Operators lose HP constantly.

stages.name: The Night of Ritual
stages.desc: The mysterious ceremony has given these Sarkaz soldiers unprecedented strength by harnessing the red moon's power. At the very last moment of the ceremony, you knock over a candle on the altar.

stages.name: The Biting Cold
stages.desc:
The ice-blue altar twinkles bright, inviting all astray travelers to lie down for a brief respite. As soon as the resting souls are completely frozen, the thugs hiding in the dark will emerge to shatter them into pieces.
 Periodically releases a frigid wave that will inflict Cold on affected allied units

stages.name: Dangers Abound
stages.desc:
Spirits are abound, and the mechanisms here continue their endless rumble. Resistance is futile, for there is nowhere to run underneath the red moon.
 Periodically fire bolts, dealing Physical Damage to our units.

stages.name: Surprise Factory
stages.desc: Some lucky audience members who got their heads smacked by the 'Small Surprises' have 'voluntarily' come to participate in the making of these airdrop gifts. They don't have the mental capacity to think much more, as all they want to do now is make more 'Small Surprises' and share the 'joy' with everyone.

stages.name: Absurd Trickeries
stages.desc:
The fearless knights charge forward, firing the enthusiasm of the audience. The unclaimed riches twinkle under the spotlight, luring those blinded by the sight into the abyss.
 Operators deployed on it have decreased DEF

stages.name: From Afar
stages.desc:
There will always be people who like to watch quietly from afar as tragedy unfolds. 
There is  that greatly increases ATK and ASPD, at the cost of gradual HP loss.

stages.name: Sarkaz Desire
stages.desc:
This ragtag team is the embodiment of the Sarkaz—Orderless, fearless, and ruthless.
 Operators deployed on it and enemies who have passed it will take constant damage, but their ATK and ASPD will be increased significantly

stages.name: Ursus Desire
stages.desc: This ragtag team is the embodiment of Ursus—Large, strong, and imposing. They desire conquest.
