stages.name: Ursus Desire
stages.desc: This ragtag team is the embodiment of Ursus—Large, strong, and imposing. They desire conquest.
stages.eliteDesc: Condition: More elite enemies will appear, and enemies have increased ATK, DEF, and HP.

stages.name: Mind the Doors
stages.desc: The spire caster's abandoned trinkets were modified, eventually becoming the castle's indefatigable guardian. At the same time, it has also proven to be the some of the new actors' barrier to entry.

stages.name: Theft from Above
stages.desc:
A flying bandit just made off with the Troupe's treasured prize, and you just so happen to be in his path of escape.
 Operators deployed on this tile have slightly reduced ASPD but greatly increased ATK when attacking Aerial units

stages.name: Terrifying Legends
stages.desc:
You see a violent, completely mutated Infected. He howls and attacks you, reminding you of a particular, and terrifying, Rim Billiton legend.
His ATK and ASPD increase as he continuously attacks

stages.name: Fatal Melodies
stages.desc:
The puppet plays his instrument unceasingly, filling the hall with a gentle melody. Yet, when you listen carefully, you notice that, behind the melody, there is an ear-piercing noise that attempts to seize the will of a listener.
When not blocked, continuously deals Arts damage to all allied units

stages.name: A Cold Separation
stages.desc:
A tragic tale is being performed on the stage right this moment—a Snowpriest and her bodyguard are separated from each other. They want to share the information they have with each other, but things are bound to never go their way.
When either of the two is incapacitated, the other will inherit all abilities of the fallen and see their combat abilities increased significantly

stages.name: The Point of No Return
stages.desc:
Rescue him. 
 Block the enemy’s route

stages.name: All I Ask of You
stages.desc: The kindhearted souls are being coerced to do wrong by the fanatics. The very sight brings you to tears, and it's time to put things right.

stages.name: Twisted Every Way
stages.desc:
You have completely ruined the host's grand performance, and he wants nothing more than to end you right here and now. With all that you have been through on this journey, you feel the same toward him.
Natural DP regeneration is greatly reduced; <'Host's Assistant’> Nearby allies have reduced ATK and continuously receive True damage.

stages.name: New Chapter
stages.desc: Throw the abandoned draft into the fire. Take out a new sheet of paper, and begin anew.

stages.name: 'Knights' Duel'
stages.desc: 'I heard there are basically two types of knights these days. Those that spell profit and those that don't. Which type are these four, do you think?'

stages.name: Demonic Cage
stages.desc: The terrifying pureblood Vampire disappears into the shadows of the castle with a maniacal laugh, leaving behind her twisted, shadowy servant.

stages.name: Disorderly Banquet
stages.desc: Art is merely a convenient excuse. What they truly want to do is to strip away what little humanity there is left in them with spices and alcohol.

stages.name: Bound by Self
stages.desc: Alas, the pen touches page, but all that is produced is hogwash.

stages.name: Observation
stages.desc: 'I want to know your motivation. What would you do under these circumstances?'

stages.name: Duck Lord's Play
stages.desc:
The famous Columbian merchant Duck Lord runs amok on the stage, scattering Originium Ingots everywhere. Let's get him out of here!
Operators deployed on it and enemies who have passed it will take constant damage, but their ATK and ASPD will be increased significantly

stages.name: Duck Lord's Party
stages.desc:
Famous Columbian merchant Duck Lord seems to have some information about the castle. Don't let him get away!
 Operators deployed on it and enemies who have passed it will take constant damage, but their ATK and ASPD will be increased significantly

stages.name: Gopnik's Fist
stages.desc:
The only thing is, the famous Ursus businessman, has challenged you, and the rewards are sure to be rich. 
 Operators deployed on it and enemies who have passed it will take constant damage, but their ATK and ASPD will be increased significantly

stages.name: This Ursus Man
stages.desc:
There are those who try to forget their pasts and live a dignified life, then there are those who cannot escape the cluelessness and pain they have been through, like this Ursus man.
 Operators deployed on it and enemies who have passed it will take constant damage, but their ATK and ASPD will be increased significantly

enemy_1007_slime.name: Originium Slug
enemy_1007_slime.desc: An infected wild creature. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_1007_slime_2.name: Originium Slug α
enemy_1007_slime_2.desc: An infected wild creature that is more dangerous than a normal Originium Slug. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_1004_mslime.name: Acid Originium Slug
enemy_1004_mslime.desc: An infected wild creature that can secrete corrosive fluids strong enough to dissolve armor. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.
enemy_1004_mslime.ability: Every attack reduces the enemy's DEF

enemy_1101_plkght.name: Roar Knightclub Trainee
enemy_1101_plkght.desc: Wearing the high-performance, lightweight armor provided by Roar Guards, this new batch of experimental armor has some humorous deficiencies that made these people into pitiful test subjects. 'Roar Guards, even the winds will bow to you!'
enemy_1101_plkght.ability: Has significantly increased DEF and RES for a period of time

enemy_2001_duckmi.name: Duck Lord
enemy_2001_duckmi.desc: A famous Columbian investor, entrepreneur, and master of 'Duck Power.' Out of the public eye, enjoys kicking back with others...
enemy_2001_duckmi.ability:
Cannot be blocked.
Gains significantly increased Movement Speed after being attacked

enemy_2002_bearmi.name: Gopnik
enemy_2002_bearmi.desc: The Ursus security guard hired by Duck Lord. Powerful, but not very smart. Mainly responsible for carrying luggage for Duck Lord.

enemy_1080_sotidp_2.name: Guerrilla Herald Leader
enemy_1080_sotidp_2.desc: Compared to regular heralds, these leaders have greater individual combat capabilities. The heralds are important links within their communication system. While present, they will issue tactical commands to increase the offensive and defensive abilities of nearby allies.
enemy_1080_sotidp_2.ability: Increases the ATK and DEF of enemy units when on the battlefield.

enemy_1000_gopro.name: Hound
enemy_1000_gopro.desc: A creature controlled by a Reunion recon squad. Monitoring equipment is strapped to its body. Due to their speed and evasiveness, they can easily slip through enemy defenses and cause heavy damage.

enemy_1084_sotidm.name: Sarkaz Guerrilla Fighter
enemy_1084_sotidm.desc: A Sarkaz fighter who supports Patriot but cannot understand the special terminology of their guerilla operations. The Sarkaz ritual maintained by the Sarkaz Originium Altars will not damage them, instead strengthening their bodies and converting their attacks into Arts damage.
enemy_1084_sotidm.ability: When coming into contact with a Pulse Wave, this unit instead takes no damage and will deal Arts damage with normal attacks.

enemy_1087_ltwolf.name: Winterwisp Hound
enemy_1087_ltwolf.desc: A hunting hound trained and utilized by locals since ancient times. For early aboriginals and pioneers, these hounds were considered invaluable partners.

enemy_1077_sotihd.name: Guerrilla Hound
enemy_1077_sotihd.desc: A basic guerrilla-ops war hound equipped with lightweight Sarkaz armor, having decent defenses while still being able to move quickly.

enemy_1034_laxe.name: Butcher
enemy_1034_laxe.desc: An enemy of unknown identity who wears white industrial overalls and attacks with a massive blunt object. Their appearances and combat style readily reminds people of those serial killers from movies, hence their nickname - Butcher.

enemy_1002_nsabr.name: Soldier
enemy_1002_nsabr.desc: One of Reunion's basic melee soldiers. Equipped with illegally obtained weapons and armor, they wear masks to conceal their identities during operations.

enemy_1024_mortar.name: Mortar Gunner
enemy_1024_mortar.desc: One of Reunion's special ranged soldiers. Equipped with pilfered military-grade Originium launchers, they launch explosive attacks from hard-to-defend positions, making them a significant threat to back-line Operators.
enemy_1024_mortar.ability:
Capable of extremely long ranged-attacks
Shells deal area damage to target and any allied units in the surrounding 8 tiles

enemy_1024_mortar_2.name: Mortar Gunner Leader
enemy_1024_mortar_2.desc: One of Reunion's special ranged soldiers, more dangerous than a normal Mortar Gunner. Equipped with pilfered military-grade Originium launchers, they launch explosive attacks from hard-to-defend positions, making them a significant threat to back-line Operators.
enemy_1024_mortar_2.ability:
Capable of extremely long ranged-attacks
Shells deal area damage to target and any allied units in the surrounding 8 tiles

enemy_1021_bslime.name: Infused Originium Slug
enemy_1021_bslime.desc: An infected wild creature that has become unstable due to assimilating a large amount of Originium into its abdomen, exploding upon death. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.
enemy_1021_bslime.ability: Death deals a large amount of Physical damage to surrounding units

enemy_1013_airdrp.name: Airborne Soldier
enemy_1013_airdrp.desc: One of Reunion's special melee soldiers. They are equipped with modified disposable jetpacks made from looted parts. Despite their fairly rudimentary equipment, Reunion uses them to great effect when attacking insufficiently protected areas.
enemy_1013_airdrp.ability: Able to land directly onto the battlefield.

enemy_1016_diaman.name: Metal Crab
enemy_1016_diaman.desc: An infected wild creature, completely covered in metal. Though most conventional weapons are not very effective against them, they aren't as heavy as they seem, and also move slowly. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_1028_mocock.name: Cocktail Thrower
enemy_1028_mocock.desc: An enemy combatant of unknown identity. They wear a face mask to conceal their identity, and use Molotov cocktails to deal damage within a range. These weapons are usually associated with riots.

enemy_1109_uabone.name: Ursus Assault Crossbowman
enemy_1109_uabone.desc: An Ursus soldier who is proficient at infiltrating deep into enemy territory. Equipped with a powerful heavy-duty crossbow.
enemy_1109_uabone.ability: Able to land directly onto the battlefield.

enemy_1056_ganwar.name: Brawler
enemy_1056_ganwar.desc: Combat personnel who have underwent special training. Their flurry of rapid blows are powerful enough to be fatal.

enemy_2010_csdcr.name: Scarlet Singer
enemy_2010_csdcr.desc: A performer of the Crimson Troupe, they unleash their singing voices when sufficiently stimulated, inciting their followers to attack in a frenzy.
enemy_2010_csdcr.ability:
Attacks inflict additional Nervous Impairment
After receiving a certain number of hits, greatly increases all enemies' ASPD for a short period

enemy_1095_ccripr.name: Tiacauh Fanatic
enemy_1095_ccripr.desc: A fervent Tiacauh warrior with very fast attack speed. Though each strike is not particularly powerful, those caught off guard will be quickly subdued by a rain of blows.
enemy_1095_ccripr.ability: Every attack reduces the enemy's DEF

enemy_1118_lidbox.name: Pugilist Prisoner
enemy_1118_lidbox.desc: An inmate who puts his trust in his fists. ——In reality, the metal chains on his wrists are doing most of the work.
enemy_1118_lidbox.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK and attacks ignore a certain portion of the target's DEF

enemy_1056_ganwar_2.name: Elite Brawler
enemy_1056_ganwar_2.desc: Exceptionally dangerous combatants who are even more destructive than normal Brawlers. Only the most heavily-armored Operators can withstand their blows.

enemy_1055_ganman.name: Sicilian
enemy_1055_ganman.desc: A basic melee combatant. These Siracusans adorn themselves with an ancient name, symbolizing the glory of an era bygone.

enemy_1008_ghost.name: Wraith
enemy_1008_ghost.desc: One of Reunion's engineers. Though unarmed, they utilize their agility and cunning to slip through defenses in order to sabotage mechanical and electrical equipment.

enemy_1026_aghost.name: Wraith Leader
enemy_1026_aghost.desc: One of Reunion's engineers. Due to being better trained and more heavily equipped, they are harder to neutralize in time, even if they have been detected. They utilize their agility and cunning to slip through defenses in order to sabotage mechanical and electrical equipment.

enemy_1007_slime_3.name: Originium Slug β
enemy_1007_slime_3.desc: An infected wild creature that is more dangerous than an Originium Slug α. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_1080_sotidp.name: Guerrilla Herald
enemy_1080_sotidp.desc: Patriot's guerilla fighters employ a special language to communicate and synergize their coordinated operations on the battlefield. The heralds are important links within their communication system. While present, they will issue tactical commands to increase the offensive and defensive abilities of nearby allies.
enemy_1080_sotidp.ability: Increases the ATK and DEF of enemy units when on the battlefield.

enemy_1078_sotisc.name: Guerrilla Fighter
enemy_1078_sotisc.desc: A basic guerilla fighter. After receiving tactical commands, they will gain increased capabilities and utilize a full range of offensive maneuvers.

enemy_1079_sotisp.name: Guerrilla Sniper
enemy_1079_sotisp.desc: A basic guerilla sniper who has received professional, military-grade sharpshooting skills. After receiving tactical commands, they will gain increased capabilities and attack two targets at once.
enemy_1079_sotisp.ability: When Inspired, attacks one additional target.

enemy_1075_dmgswd_2.name: Sarkaz Bladeweaver Leader
enemy_1075_dmgswd_2.desc: An elite Sarkaz mercenary who has mastered the combination of swordsmanship and Arts. The Originium flowing through their blood allows them to hold these crudely modified Arts-imbued greatswords. Though they won't attack while on Standby, they are capable of dealing melee attacks that inflict Arts damage while in Combat-Ready mode.
enemy_1075_dmgswd_2.ability: Starts battle in Standby and will not attack; upon being injured, becomes Combat-Ready and inflicts Arts damage with melee attacks.

enemy_1025_reveng.name: Avenger
enemy_1025_reveng.desc: An unidentified combatant who attacks using unconventional methods. Equipped with a special weapon, they are known to change their attack patterns during combat, making them difficult to defend against.
enemy_1025_reveng.ability: Gains significantly increased ATK when HP is under 50%

enemy_1098_cchmpn.name: Tiacauh Brave
enemy_1098_cchmpn.desc: A very formidable Tiacauh warrior, naturally born to do battle. These warriors harbor great pride in their combat abilities, and live for the thrill of the duel no matter where they are. Their single-mindedness allows them to shrug off all outside interference.
enemy_1098_cchmpn.ability: When blocked, this unit will only receive damage from the unit blocking it, and the DEF of both units is decreased.

enemy_1000_gopro_2.name: Hound Pro
enemy_1000_gopro_2.desc: A creature controlled by a Reunion recon squad that is more dangerous than a normal Hound. Monitoring equipment is strapped to its body. Due to their speed and evasiveness, they can easily slip through enemy defenses and cause heavy damage.

enemy_1015_litamr.name: Light-Armored Soldier
enemy_1015_litamr.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers. Equipped with illegally obtained weapons and armor, they are better equipped and trained, and cannot be taken down with conventional strategies.

enemy_1099_nbkght.name: Nameless Independent Knight
enemy_1099_nbkght.desc: An independent Kazimierz knight. A low profile and unrefined skill means this one will soon be swept away in the tide of knight competition.

enemy_1100_scorpn.name: Training Gloompincer
enemy_1100_scorpn.desc: Infected creatures commonly found in the great lakes and rivers of Kazimierz, captured and raised by casters and used as tools to train knights and entertain fight spectators.

enemy_1018_aoemag.name: Senior Caster
enemy_1018_aoemag.desc: One of Reunion's elite Casters. They use complex Area-of-Effect Arts, making them more potent than normal Casters. Proceed with caution.
enemy_1018_aoemag.ability: Deals area damage to Operators on the four cardinal tiles next to the primary target.

enemy_1103_wdkght.name: Bloodboil Knightclub Trainee
enemy_1103_wdkght.desc: A trainee from the Bloodboil Knightclub. The Bloodboil Knights have long been known for their ferocity and aggression, from trainees to knight nobles. Whenever one of their brothers falls, they gain courage and combat prowess.
enemy_1103_wdkght.ability: Each time an enemy is defeated, gains ATK and ASPD, stacking up to 10 times.

enemy_1103_wdkght_2.name: Bloodboil Knightclub Elite
enemy_1103_wdkght_2.desc: A regular knight from the Bloodboil Knightclub. The Bloodboil Knights have long been known for their ferocity and aggression, from trainees to knight nobles. Whenever one of their brothers falls, they gain courage and combat prowess.
enemy_1103_wdkght_2.ability: Each time an enemy is defeated, gains ATK and ASPD, stacking up to 10 times.

enemy_1036_amraoe.name: Shielded Senior Caster
enemy_1036_amraoe.desc: One of Reunion's elite Casters. They use complex Area-of-Effect Arts, making them more potent than normal Casters. They also seem to have fortified their defenses with Arts. and must be handled with caution. Due to their special state, they may be more vulnerable to Arts damage.
enemy_1036_amraoe.ability: Deals area damage to Operators on the four cardinal tiles next to the primary target.

enemy_1039_breakr.name: Breaker
enemy_1039_breakr.desc: A well-trained enemy unit that spearheads assaults. Though their attack power is only slightly higher than average, their terrifying speed often allows them to break through the defenses of the unprepared- hence their nickname, 'Breakers.'

enemy_1502_crowns.name: Crownslayer
enemy_1502_crowns.desc: One of Reunion's squad leaders who handles infiltration and assassination operations, specializing in melee combat and breaking through defensive lines. Her stealth and superb infiltration abilities have foiled countless attempts to capture her.
enemy_1502_crowns.ability: When blocked, can quickly move behind the unit

enemy_1038_lunmag.name: Spec Ops Caster
enemy_1038_lunmag.desc: One of Reunion's Casters. Their armor makes them more resistant to physical damage compared to regular Casters.

enemy_1029_shdsbr.name: Shielded Soldier
enemy_1029_shdsbr.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers. Equipped with illegally obtained weapons and armor, they additionally bear a shield. They are slightly harder to take down than a regular Reunion foot soldier.

enemy_1038_lunmag_2.name: Spec Ops Caster Leader
enemy_1038_lunmag_2.desc: One of Reunion's elite Casters. Their armor makes them more resistant to physical damage compared to regular Casters. They have mastered Arts that allows them to attack two targets at once. Proceed with caution.

enemy_1081_sotisd.name: Guerrilla Shieldguard
enemy_1081_sotisd.desc: One of Patriot's elite defenders. Carrying out Patriot's will, these shieldguards form the solid foundation of the 'Aegis of the Infected.' Equipped with high-performance protective armor, they cover for their comrades by drawing fire from enemies while present on the battlefield.
enemy_1081_sotisd.ability: More likely to be targeted by attacks.

enemy_1111_ucommd_2.name: Ursus Raider Leader
enemy_1111_ucommd_2.desc: Captains within the Ursus Army that strike a balance between mobility and breakthrough capabilities, equipped with higher-quality armor. Good at fighting underequipped enemy forces.
enemy_1111_ucommd_2.ability:
Can only be blocked by units with at least 2 Block
Can use long-ranged weapons to attack  and

enemy_1107_uoffcr.name: Infected Patrol Captain
enemy_1107_uoffcr.desc: An officer tasked with hunting the Infected of Ursus. Greedy and cold-blooded.
enemy_1107_uoffcr.ability: Can use long-ranged weapons to attack  and

enemy_1107_uoffcr_2.name: Elite Infected Patrol Captain
enemy_1107_uoffcr_2.desc: A high-ranking officer tasked with hunting the Infected of Ursus. Greedy and cold-blooded. They are not a part of the army, and are generally despised by Ursus soldiers and city officials alike.
enemy_1107_uoffcr_2.ability: Can use long-ranged weapons to attack  and

enemy_1109_uabone_2.name: Elite Ursus Assault Crossbowman
enemy_1109_uabone_2.desc: An Ursus soldier who is exceptionally proficient at infiltrating deep into enemy territory. Equipped with an extremely powerful heavy-duty crossbow.
enemy_1109_uabone_2.ability: Able to land directly onto the battlefield.

enemy_1111_ucommd.name: Ursus Raider
enemy_1111_ucommd.desc: Special forces within the Ursus Army that strike a balance between mobility and breakthrough capabilities. Good at fighting underequipped enemy forces.
enemy_1111_ucommd.ability:
Can only be blocked by units with at least 2 Block
Can use long-ranged weapons to attack  and

enemy_1110_uamord.name: Ursus Armored Caster
enemy_1110_uamord.desc: A basic caster in the Ursus Army. Clad in simple armor.

enemy_3001_upeopl.name: Ursus Civilian
enemy_3001_upeopl.desc: An ordinary Ursus citizen caught in the crossfire. A part of the cruelty and suffering of this world.
enemy_3001_upeopl.ability:
Does not deplete Life Points when spared, but will deplete a Life Point upon dying
Does not count towards the defeated enemies counter

enemy_1058_traink.name: Bully
enemy_1058_traink.desc: Special melee combatants. Their unstoppable bulk is equivalent to blocking three normal enemies, and Operators who cannot block three enemies may not be able to halt their advance at all.
enemy_1058_traink.ability: Can only be blocked by Operators with 3 or more Block

enemy_1504_cqbw.name: W
enemy_1504_cqbw.desc: A Sarkaz Mercenary and one of Reunion's squad leaders, she carries around a weapon that resembles a Laterano Gun. However, she almost exclusively attacks using explosives and throwing weapons. One of her common strategies is to attack the enemy's flank using large amounts of active Originium explosives. However, her erratic movements make her almost impossible to predict...
enemy_1504_cqbw.ability:
Can use explosives to deal heavy Physical damage to allied units
Uses more explosives when HP is under 50%

enemy_1073_dscout.name: Sarkaz Sentinel
enemy_1073_dscout.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary. They are responsible for scouting, patrolling, and issuing early warnings to the entire squad. Once attacked, the Herald will immediately issue a warning that Inspires the entire army, shifting allied Sarkaz on Standby into Combat-Ready mode.
enemy_1073_dscout.ability: When injured, issues an alarm to immediately enhance the ATK and DEF of all enemy units, and makes enemies in Standby immediately enter Combat-Ready mode

enemy_1071_dftman.name: Sarkaz Swordsman
enemy_1071_dftman.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary. Though scarred by years of war, they are still fighting to fulfill the orders of their employers.

enemy_1075_dmgswd.name: Sarkaz Bladeweaver
enemy_1075_dmgswd.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary. The Originium flowing through their blood allows them to hold these crudely modified Arts-imbued greatswords. Though they won't attack while on Standby, they are capable of dealing melee attacks that inflict Arts damage while in Combat-Ready mode.
enemy_1075_dmgswd.ability: Starts battle in Standby and will not attack; upon being injured, becomes Combat-Ready and inflicts Arts damage with melee attacks.

enemy_1021_bslime_2.name: Infused Originium Slug α
enemy_1021_bslime_2.desc: An infected wild creature that has become unstable due to assimilating a large amount of Originium into its abdomen, exploding upon death. More dangerous than a normal Infused Originium Slug. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.
enemy_1021_bslime_2.ability: Death deals a large amount of Physical damage to surrounding units

enemy_1009_lurker.name: Lurker
enemy_1009_lurker.desc: One of Reunion's special soldiers. They move swiftly and stealthily, making it almost impossible to detect them before they engage in combat. Therefore, teamwork is needed in order to defeat them.

enemy_1001_bigbo.name: Big Bob
enemy_1001_bigbo.desc: One of Reunion's squad leaders. He wears a heavy blast suit and attacks with an electric chainsaw. He has been spotted leading peripheral operations in some remote areas, and is known to be a seasoned combatant and commander. Due to his devastating attacks and extreme survivability, proceed with utmost caution.

enemy_1005_yokai.name: Monster
enemy_1005_yokai.desc: An unmanned enemy drone, called 'Monster' by Reunion forces. Equipped with a long-range Arts receiver, they are likely operated by concealed Casters. Though unlikely to cause heavy damage to armored Operators, they can still pose a considerable threat.

enemy_1040_bombd_2.name: Bombtail-G
enemy_1040_bombd_2.desc: An unmanned enemy drone that flies slowly. They carry a large explosive warhead that is launched when approaching nearby Operators, inflicting physical damage in an area. Their design seems to have been inspired by a fantasy creature. Though they cannot attack anymore after launching the warhead, their movement speed increases afterwards due to the loss of weight.
enemy_1040_bombd_2.ability:
Flying unit.
Can launch a warhead to deal AoE physical damage
Gains significantly increased Movement Speed after launching warhead

enemy_1046_agent.name: Infantry
enemy_1046_agent.desc: A basic melee soldier. Their armor provides some defense against Arts damage.

enemy_1006_shield.name: Heavy Defender
enemy_1006_shield.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers. Equipped with a full set of heavy armor, they have ample combat experience and are extremely hard to defeat. As they may have had formal training, proceed with caution.

enemy_1000_gopro_3.name: Rabid Hound Pro
enemy_1000_gopro_3.desc: A creature controlled by a Reunion recon squad that is more dangerous than a Hound Pro. Monitoring equipment is strapped to its body. Due to their speed and evasiveness, they can easily slip through enemy defenses and cause heavy damage.

enemy_1003_ncbow_2.name: Crossbowman Leader
enemy_1003_ncbow_2.desc: One of Reunion's senior ranged soldiers, more powerful than a normal Crossbowman. Equipped with illegally obtained weapons and armor, they wear masks to conceal their identities during operations.

enemy_1033_handax_2.name: Veteran Logger
enemy_1033_handax_2.desc: An enemy of unknown identity who wears logging equipment and attacks with lumbering tools. They dye their clothes red to make it easier to identify them, but are also harder to deal with. Their nickname is a bit more human-sounding than 'Log Splitter,' but humans are more terrifying than any machine.

enemy_1032_katar.name: Bladed Fighter
enemy_1032_katar.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers who has been trained in traditional martial arts. Equipped with refitted fist weapons, they are capable of inflicting massive damage with their rapid strikes.

enemy_1030_wteeth.name: Junkman
enemy_1030_wteeth.desc: A ragged-looking enemy combatant. According to intel, they disguise themselves as drifters to conceal the fact that they are mercenaries. Though their background is unknown, their fierce attacks require them to be dealt with cautiously.

enemy_2009_csaudc.name: Derisive Audience
enemy_2009_csaudc.desc: A diehard fan of the Crimson Troupe, they enjoy the performances night and day. They have become as incomprehensible as the troupe itself.

enemy_2012_csbln.name: 'Small Surprise'
enemy_2012_csbln.desc: A special gift prepared by the Crimson Troupe for the audience. Every audience member who receives a gift will become particularly 'ecstatic'.
enemy_2012_csbln.ability:
Flying unit.
Upon death, deals Arts damage to all surrounding units and inflicts Nervous Impairment

enemy_1116_liprr.name: Ordinary Prisoner
enemy_1116_liprr.desc: A commonly seen prisoner of Mansfield State Prison. Even so, don't let your guard down or he'll slip past you.
enemy_1116_liprr.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK

enemy_1117_liiprr.name: Sniper Prisoner
enemy_1117_liiprr.desc: An inmate who cobbled together a ranged weapon to avoid getting into brawls. Knows a little about Originium Arts and can strengthen his 'arrows'.
enemy_1117_liiprr.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK and now deals Arts damage
Prioritizes attacking Imprisonment Devices

enemy_1118_lidbox_2.name: Elite Pugilist Prisoner
enemy_1118_lidbox_2.desc: An inmate who wholeheartedly trusts in his fists. Do not underestimate this inmate who is able to utilize both fist and chain in harmony now.
enemy_1118_lidbox_2.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK and attacks ignore a certain portion of the target's DEF

enemy_1119_vofsd.name: Strongman Prisoner
enemy_1119_vofsd.desc: An inmate with great strength. The weapons in his hands seem to have been acquired from ransacking a storage room. During a melee, will often rush to the frontline and inflict great damage.
enemy_1119_vofsd.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK and RES and regenerates HP over time

enemy_1169_duphlx.name: Dublinn Phalanx Infantry
enemy_1169_duphlx.desc: A phalanx infantry in the Dublinn forces. The phalanx tactics they utilize evolved from the combat formations used in Gaul. In the phalanx, nearby infantry will support one another, increasing the might of the formation.
enemy_1169_duphlx.ability:
When other Phalanx Infantry are nearby, DEF increases (stacks)
Refraction

enemy_1169_duphlx_2.name: Dublinn Phalanx Commander
enemy_1169_duphlx_2.desc: A phalanx commander in the Dublinn forces, they organize the infantry under their command into phalanx formations. The phalanx tactics they utilize evolved from the combat formations used in Gaul. In the phalanx, nearby infantry will support one another, increasing the might of the formation.
enemy_1169_duphlx_2.ability:
When other Phalanx Commanders are nearby, DEF increases (stacks)
Refraction

enemy_1172_dugago.name: Tombkeeper Grotesque
enemy_1172_dugago.desc: A special Arts creation from the Dublinn forces, created by their leader Mandragora. According to Mandragora, back when she was studying Originium Arts in an outskirts cemetery, the Tombkeeper Grotesque was her first companion. Its outer appearance has gone through many redesigns, with the latest being based off a Sarkaz from an ancient bloodline.
enemy_1172_dugago.ability:
After first defeat, temporarily transforms into a stone statue with extremely high DEF, then revives as an aerial unit
Refraction

enemy_1015_litamr_2.name: Light-Armored Soldier Leader
enemy_1015_litamr_2.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers, more dangerous than a normal Armored Soldier. Equipped with illegally obtained weapons and armor, they are better equipped and trained, and cannot be taken down with conventional strategies.

enemy_1045_hammer.name: Defense Crusher
enemy_1045_hammer.desc: One of Reunion's senior elites, clad in a heavy blast suit. Each swing from their weapon is slow but packs tremendous destructive power. Operators will be stunned after taking several attacks.Proceed with caution.
enemy_1045_hammer.ability: After two attacks, the next attack Stuns its target

enemy_1041_lazerd_2.name: Arts Master A2
enemy_1041_lazerd_2.desc: An unmanned enemy drone that had some of its Flight Support modules swapped out, trading speed for a more powerful Arts Weapon module. Capable of using long-range Arts attacks. Proceed with caution. Based on available data, its weapon module also allows it to block some Arts damage.

enemy_1039_breakr_2.name: Breaker Leader
enemy_1039_breakr_2.desc: A well-trained enemy unit that spearheads assaults, and is harder to defeat than a normal Breaker. Their attack power is higher than average, and their movement speed is incredibly high.

enemy_1031_mrogue_2.name: Arts Guard Leader
enemy_1031_mrogue_2.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers who also has been trained in using Originium Arts, more dangerous than a normal Arts Guard. They are able to enhance their melee attacks with armor-ignoring Arts damage. Reunion's frontline squads have become a lot more devastating since the inclusion of these soldiers.

enemy_1076_bsthmr_2.name: Demolitionist Leader
enemy_1076_bsthmr_2.desc: A high-level elite Reunion combatant who wears heavy-duty, explosion-proof protective gear, more aggressive than normal Demolitionists. Their special Originium-powered hammers have massive destructive range, and must be dealt with cautiously.
enemy_1076_bsthmr_2.ability: Attacks cause an explosion, dealing Splash damage to units around the primary target.

enemy_1025_reveng_2.name: Hateful Avenger
enemy_1025_reveng_2.desc: An unidentified combatant who attacks using unconventional methods, more dangerous than a normal Avenger. Equipped with a special weapon, they are known to change their attack patterns during combat, making them difficult to defend against. Proceed with caution.
enemy_1025_reveng_2.ability: Gains significantly increased ATK when HP is under 50%

enemy_1011_wizard_2.name: Caster Leader
enemy_1011_wizard_2.desc: One of Reunion's senior Casters, more powerful than a normal Caster. These Infected casters tend to use their own Originium to power their Arts. Because they don't rely on staves, their attacks are more unpredictable and they cannot simply be disarmed.

enemy_1509_mousek.name: 'Rat King'
enemy_1509_mousek.desc: Lin, Lin Gray, the Rat King of the slum. He bears many titles, just like the shifting of the city's shadows underneath his feet. Don't touch the red lines. If you ever have a moment of indecisiveness, whatever it is that you were concerned about will become nothing more than a whisper in the winds above the dunes.
enemy_1509_mousek.ability:
[Singing Sands] Targets a unit with the highest Max HP, dealing heavy physical damage to the target and nearby allied units within a cross area
[Sand Tomb] Targets a unit with the lowest Max HP, continuously inflicting Arts damage to the target and nearby allies, also reducing their ATK
Appears with a Barrier that can absorb a large amount of Arts damage, gains significantly increased DEF while Barrier is active
Deals more damage when HP drops below half

enemy_1143_merrpg.name: Anti-Armor Infantry
enemy_1143_merrpg.desc: A mercenary specializing in anti-armor combat. Carries a single-use grenade launcher which is discarded after firing along with other non-essential equipment, after which they charge at high speed towards the enemy.
enemy_1143_merrpg.ability:
Can fire a super long-range grenade, dealing AoE Physical damage
After firing, significantly increases Movement Speed and can only perform melee attacks

enemy_1143_merrpg_2.name: Anti-Armor Infantry Leader
enemy_1143_merrpg_2.desc: A mercenary specializing in anti-armor combat. Carries a powerful single-use grenade launcher which is discarded after firing along with other non-essential equipment, after which they charge at high speed towards the enemy.
enemy_1143_merrpg_2.ability:
Can fire a super long-range grenade, dealing AoE Physical damage
After firing, significantly increases Movement Speed and can only perform melee attacks

enemy_1072_dlancer.name: Sarkaz Lancer
enemy_1072_dlancer.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary. After special training, they are capable of rushing forward with a long spear, building up speed. The faster they charge, the harder they strike anyone who might stand in their way.
enemy_1072_dlancer.ability: Gradually accelerates when moving, and deals additional damage on the first attack after being blocked based upon Movement Speed.

enemy_1072_dlancer_2.name: Sarkaz Lancer Leader
enemy_1072_dlancer_2.desc: A Sarkaz, more threatening than a normal Sarkaz Lancer. After special training, they are capable of rushing forward with a long spear, building up speed. The faster they charge, the harder they strike anyone who might stand in their way.
enemy_1072_dlancer_2.ability: Gradually accelerates when moving, and deals additional damage on the first attack after being blocked based upon Movement Speed.

enemy_1165_duhond.name: Dublinn Sniffer Hound
enemy_1165_duhond.desc: An organism controlled by the Dublinn forces. They were originally purchased from a mercenary group. Equipped with a refractive mask crafted by Shadowcasters, they are rather resistant to regular Originium Arts.

enemy_1171_durokt.name: Dublinn Flying Soldier
enemy_1171_durokt.desc: A flying soldier in the Dublinn forces. The steam propulsion device they use originates from a secret laboratory owned by the Victorian military. As it has only recently been deployed in battle, its power output is still unstable.

enemy_1171_durokt_2.name: Dublinn Flying Squad Leader
enemy_1171_durokt_2.desc: A flying squad leader in the Dublinn forces. The steam propulsion device they use originates from a secret laboratory owned by the Victorian military and has gone through many iterations. Its power output is relatively stable.

enemy_1170_dushld.name: Dublinn Heavy Defender
enemy_1170_dushld.desc: A heavy defender in the Dublinn forces. The armor technology they use comes from Victoria, while their boots were designed in Leithanien. Their shields look extremely similar to a newly patented product from Kazimierz's Roar Guards Company.

enemy_1170_dushld_2.name: Dublinn Heavy Defender Leader
enemy_1170_dushld_2.desc: A heavy defender leader in the Dublinn forces, they wield much more sophisticated equipment. The armor technology they use comes from Victoria, while their boots were designed in Leithanien. Their shields look extremely similar to a newly patented product from Kazimierz's Roar Guards Company.

enemy_1176_dusocr.name: Dublinn Evocator
enemy_1176_dusocr.desc: An evocation caster in the Dublinn forces who can control fireballs. Their leader's teachings reverberate in their minds as they work to purify the corruption of Terra in raging flames.
enemy_1176_dusocr.ability: Controls  that move along the ground and explode after hitting a melee ally unit, dealing Arts damage and Burn; damage dealt decreases with distance moved

enemy_1176_dusocr_2.name: Dublinn Evocator Leader
enemy_1176_dusocr_2.desc: An evocation caster leader in the Dublinn forces who can control fireballs, they are more adept at using Originium Arts than their underlings. Their leader's teachings reverberate in their minds as they work to purify the corruption of Terra in raging flames.
enemy_1176_dusocr_2.ability: Controls  that move along the ground and explode after hitting a melee ally unit, dealing Arts damage and Burn; damage dealt decreases with distance moved

enemy_1005_yokai_2.name: Monster Mk II
enemy_1005_yokai_2.desc: An unmanned enemy drone that has been upgraded from its namesake model, the Monster MkII. Equipped with a small gun and a long-range Arts receiver, they are likely operated by concealed Casters. Though unlikely to cause heavy damage to armored Operators, they can still pose a considerable threat.

enemy_1017_defdrn.name: Defender-4
enemy_1017_defdrn.desc: An unmanned enemy drone known as the Defender-4. Though they do not have any offensive capabilities, they must be taken care of due to their ability to support enemy troops.
enemy_1017_defdrn.ability:
Flying unit.
Increases the DEF of surrounding enemy units

enemy_2011_csppt.name: Theater Golem
enemy_2011_csppt.desc: A gigantic golem maintained by the Crimson Troupe's prop department, they are normally used to move bulky items. They are massive and difficult to block.
enemy_2011_csppt.ability:
Can only be blocked by units with at least 4 Block
Attacks deal Splash damage to allied units in a small area around self

enemy_1191_krgrg.name: Icefield Arts Fighter
enemy_1191_krgrg.desc: A special warrior born in a harsh environment. While they mainly use Arts in combat, their bodies are simply another one of their weapons. They are at their most dangerous when they fall in battle.
enemy_1191_krgrg.ability:
When defeated, deals Arts damage to all surrounding units and inflicts Cold
Loses HP when shifted

enemy_1189_krgaxe.name: Icefield Warrior
enemy_1189_krgaxe.desc: A warrior proficient in icefield combat. Long periods of living on the icefields have tempered their will and physique.

enemy_1187_krghd.name: Clawbeast
enemy_1187_krghd.desc: A wild beast that began appearing in the wilderness several decades ago. Depending on differences in the environment, their bodies and organs undergo various changes.

enemy_1193_krgbsk_2.name: Icefield Berserker Leader
enemy_1193_krgbsk_2.desc: A berserker of the icefields. The blistering cold brings them joy, and glacial winds do naught but excite them. To be able to fell their enemies in the tempestuous snow is their greatest bliss.
enemy_1193_krgbsk_2.ability:
A berserker of the icefields whose attack power greatly increases when attacking Frozen targets
Gains status resistance

enemy_1005_yokai_3.name: Raptor
enemy_1005_yokai_3.desc: An unmanned enemy drone, its design and name seem to be inspired by a certain space game. Equipped with a minigun and a long-range Arts receiver, they are likely operated by concealed Casters. Their outstanding stealthiness and attack power pose a major threat to Operators.

enemy_1041_lazerd.name: Arts Master A1
enemy_1041_lazerd.desc: An unmanned enemy drone with extremely high movement speed, modified from the product of an Arts Education competition. It is equipped with an Arts Weapon module that allows it to launch long-range Arts attacks, so proceed with caution. Based on available data, its weapon module also allows it to block some Arts damage.

enemy_1112_emppnt_2.name: Imperial Artillery Core Targeteer
enemy_1112_emppnt_2.desc: A heavily-armored, sturdily-built core drone operated by artillery technicians of the Ursus army. They presage the withering firepower of the Ursus Artillery. The Ursus Artillery is a fire team that has bunkered down into the city. Though packing less firepower than naval guns, their equipment still shreds through buildings and armor with ease.

enemy_1501_demonk.name: Sarkaz Centurion
enemy_1501_demonk.desc: One of Reunion's squad leaders who also serves as a Sarkaz mercenary. He leads a group of Sarkaz mercenaries to fight on the front lines. Based upon the limited intel available, he is extremely tight-lipped, thus making it unlikely to get any information from him.

enemy_1049_eagent_2.name: Agent Leader
enemy_1049_eagent_2.desc: A caster who utilizes coordinated tactics. They are stronger than normal Agents. They are able to enhance their melee attacks with Arts damage, and employ a variety of techniques to break through unprepared defenders.
enemy_1049_eagent_2.ability: Attacks temporarily reduce allied units' Block

enemy_1071_dftman_2.name: Sarkaz Blademaster
enemy_1071_dftman_2.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary, more skilled than an ordinary Sarkaz Swordsman. Though scarred by years of war, they are still fighting to fulfill the orders of their employers.

enemy_1010_demon.name: Sarkaz Greatswordsman
enemy_1010_demon.desc: A mercenary from Sarkaz. The Originium inside them has boosted their physical abilities, allowing them to wield larger weapons with ease and withstand more damage.

enemy_1074_dbskar.name: Sarkaz Grudgebearer
enemy_1074_dbskar.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary. Their Originium-wracked bodies have become convenient tools in their operations, allowing them to unleash Arts without relying on a staff. Though they won't attack while on Standby, they will cruelly expend their own life to complete their mission once in Combat-Ready mode.
enemy_1074_dbskar.ability: Starts battle in Standby and will not attack; upon being injured, becomes Combat-Ready and attacks two targets with long-ranged Arts damage.

enemy_1022_dmage.name: Sarkaz Caster
enemy_1022_dmage.desc: A mercenary from Sarkaz. They use complex Arts to attack. Proceed with caution.
enemy_1022_dmage.ability: Uses chains to paralyze allied Operators.

enemy_1067_snslime.name: Infused Glacial Originium Slug
enemy_1067_snslime.desc: An infected wild creature native to colder environments that has become unstable due to assimilating a large amount of Originium into its abdomen. Upon death, they will explode and freeze nearby Operators. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by Yeti Squadron members for various tactical purposes.
enemy_1067_snslime.ability: Upon death, deals Physical damage in an area and applies Cold to affected targets

enemy_1065_snwolf.name: Frostfang
enemy_1065_snwolf.desc: Creatures manipulated by the Yeti Squadron. Their bodies are equipped with a certain brand of monitoring equipment. Due to their speed and natural camouflage in the tundra environment, they can easily slip through our defenses and inflict massive damage. At the same time, they can inflict fatal damage to frozen Operators, and must be dealt with cautiously.
enemy_1065_snwolf.ability: Attack power increases when attacking Frozen targets

enemy_1064_snsbr.name: Yeti Operative
enemy_1064_snsbr.desc: A basic melee combatants of the Yeti Squadron hailing from teams of infected guerilla warriors in the Northwestern Tundra. They are skilled at attacking frozen Operators.
enemy_1064_snsbr.ability: Attack power increases when attacking Frozen targets

enemy_1068_snmage.name: Yeti Caster
enemy_1068_snmage.desc: A basic caster of the Yeti Squadron hailing from teams of infected guerilla warriors in the Northwestern Tundra. Through special Arts and devices, they can inflict Cold on our Operators, reducing their attack speed and making them vulnerable to being Frozen.
enemy_1068_snmage.ability: After two attacks, the next attack applies the Cold effect

enemy_1070_iced.name: Oneiros
enemy_1070_iced.desc: An unmanned, slow-flying enemy drone equipped with a special warhead released upon approaching Operators that inflicts Arts damage and a freezing effect in an area. Modified from the Bombtail, they lost their ability to accelerate after delivering the payload in order to accomodate a special warhead.
enemy_1070_iced.ability:
Flying unit.
Can launch a warhead to deal AoE Arts damage and apply the Cold effect to targets

enemy_1069_icebrk.name: Yeti Icecleaver
enemy_1069_icebrk.desc: A special melee combatant of the Yeti Squadron hailing from teams of infected gerilla warriors in the Northwestern Tundra. Grim reapers on the battlefield, they wield long katanas that can easily ravage frozen Operators, hence the nickname they've been given, 'Ice Cleaver.'
enemy_1069_icebrk.ability: Attack power increases greatly when attacking Frozen targets

enemy_1014_rogue.name: Dual Swordsman
enemy_1014_rogue.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers. Trained in dual-wielding, it is likely that these fighters once served in the military. Their combat expertise makes them harder to deal with than the average rioter.

enemy_1019_jshoot_2.name: Invisible Crossbowman Leader
enemy_1019_jshoot_2.desc: One of Reunion's ranged soldiers, more dangerous than a normal Invisible Crossbowman. The ghillie suit they wear makes it almost impossible to detect them before they engage in combat. Therefore, teamwork is needed to defeat them.

enemy_1079_sotisp_2.name: Guerrilla Sniper Leader
enemy_1079_sotisp_2.desc: A veteran guerilla sniper who has extensive professional, military-grade sharpshooting skills. After receiving tactical commands, they will gain increased capabilities and attack two targets at once.
enemy_1079_sotisp_2.ability: When Inspired, attacks one additional target.

enemy_1045_hammer_2.name: Defense Crusher Leader
enemy_1045_hammer_2.desc: One of Reunion's senior elites, clad in a heavy blast suit and more dangerous than a normal Defense Crusher. Each swing from their weapon is slow but packs tremendous destructive power. Operators will be stunned after taking several attacks. Proceed with caution.
enemy_1045_hammer_2.ability: After two attacks, the next attack Stuns its target

enemy_1074_dbskar_2.name: Sarkaz Worldcurser
enemy_1074_dbskar_2.desc: An elite Sarkaz caster mercenary. Their Originium-wracked bodies have become convenient tools in their operations, and their accumulated resentment has crystallized into raw power. Though they won't attack while on Standby, they will cruelly expend their own life to complete their mission once in Combat-Ready mode.
enemy_1074_dbskar_2.ability: Starts battle in Standby and will not attack; upon being injured, becomes Combat-Ready and attacks two targets with long-ranged Arts damage.

enemy_2017_csphts.name: Ominous Apparition
enemy_2017_csphts.desc: A patch of shadow. It has never once left, nor has it ever truly existed.
enemy_2017_csphts.ability:
Has a chance to dodge Physical and Arts attacks when not blocked
Can use a skill to deal Physical damage in the surrounding area
Attacks and skills ignore a certain amount of armor and inflict additional Nervous Impairment

enemy_1112_emppnt.name: Imperial Artillery Targeteer
enemy_1112_emppnt.desc: A heavily-armored, sturdily-built drone operated by artillery casters of the Ursus army. They presage the withering firepower of the Ursus Artillery.

enemy_1108_uterer_2.name: Ursus Mangler Packmother
enemy_1108_uterer_2.desc: A warbeast pack leader trained by the Ursus Army. Obedient and relentless, they tear through armor with ease to sate their thirst for flesh and blood. Nobody has ever seen a male mangler.

enemy_1081_sotisd_2.name: Guerrilla Shieldguard Leader
enemy_1081_sotisd_2.desc: The cream of the crop among Patriot's elite defenders. After extensive training, the Shieldguard Leaders don heavier protective armor and cover for their comrades by drawing fire from enemies while present on the battlefield.
enemy_1081_sotisd_2.ability: More likely to be targeted by attacks.

enemy_2003_rockman.name: Lost Colossus
enemy_2003_rockman.desc: An experimental creation of a certain caster from the Spires of Leithanien. This colossus was originally made to be a servant, but the old Spire caster forgot to tell it who to serve…
enemy_2003_rockman.ability: Can throw a rock that inflicts a very long Stun (will not target a unit that is already Stunned)

enemy_1006_shield_2.name: Heavy Defender Leader
enemy_1006_shield_2.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers, more dangerous than a normal Heavy Defender. Equipped with a full set of heavy armor, they have ample combat experience and are extremely hard to defeat. As they may have had formal training, proceed with caution.

enemy_2004_balloon.name: Jetman
enemy_2004_balloon.desc: One of the dessert-time jokes among Columbians involves a strange man who calls himself a 'Master Thief,' puttering around with a self-made jetpack. The most valuable thing he's stolen is a single burdenbeast. However, the fact that he made his own jetpack might mean that he really is a master.
enemy_2004_balloon.ability: When blocked, this unit will take off for a period of time (this effect has a cooldown)

enemy_1023_jmage_2.name: Invisible Caster Leader
enemy_1023_jmage_2.desc: One of Reunion's Casters, more dangerous than a normal Invisible Caster. The ghillie suit they wear makes it almost impossible to detect them before they engage in combat. Therefore, teamwork is needed to defeat them.

enemy_2005_axetro.name: 'Forsaken One'
enemy_2005_axetro.desc: 'The rescuers found a security guard who had been heavily corroded by Originium... already turned into a terrifying monster... carnage everywhere... countless casualties... whereabouts unknown.' —Rim Billiton mining record
enemy_2005_axetro.ability: Continuous attacks will increase ATK and ASPD, up to a limit. This effect will reset after a few seconds after this unit stops attacking

enemy_1033_handax.name: Logger
enemy_1033_handax.desc: An enemy of unknown identity who wears logging equipment and attacks with lumbering tools. Because of their attire, weapon, and machine-like behavior, they are often called 'Log Splitters.'

enemy_1035_haxe.name: Armed Militant
enemy_1035_haxe.desc: An enemy of unknown identity. Equipped with a complex array of armor, a gas mask, and tanks of unidentifiable substances, they attack using giant blunt instruments. Due to their logic-defying equipment, they are simply called 'Armed Militants.' Though their movement is slow, even a single swing of their weapon is sure to inflict massive damage. Proceed with caution.

enemy_2013_csbot.name: Wandering Puppet
enemy_2013_csbot.desc: A musical puppet maintained by the Crimson Troupe's prop department. All who have heard its bewitching melodies have turned as one towards the Crimson Troupe, and were never seen again.
enemy_2013_csbot.ability: When not blocked, continuously deals Arts damage to all allied units

enemy_1051_norwiz.name: Authorized Caster
enemy_1051_norwiz.desc: A basic caster soldier. Arts have been one of the most threatening forms of warfare since ancient times, and a large amount of research has gone into adapting Arts for contemporary warfare.

enemy_1031_mrogue.name: Arts Guard
enemy_1031_mrogue.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers who has also been trained in using Originium Arts. They are able to enhance their melee attacks with armor-ignoring Arts damage. Reunion's frontline squads have become a lot more devastating since the inclusion of these soldiers.

enemy_2014_csicer.name: 'Snow Doe'
enemy_2014_csicer.desc: An actor playing the role of the Sami Snowpriest's bodyguard, 'Snow Doe'. After countless performances, they have fallen completely into their role, and wish for their demise at the hands of a foe as twisted as the 'Frozen Monstrosity'.
enemy_2014_csicer.ability:
Attacks inflict Cold
When <'Frost Buck'> dies, greatly increases ASPD and Movement Speed, and inherits its ability

enemy_2015_csicem.name: 'Frost Buck'
enemy_2015_csicem.desc: An actor playing the role of the Sami Snowpriest's bodyguard, 'Frost Buck'. After countless performances, they have fallen completely into their role, and wish for their demise at the hands of a foe as scary as the 'Demons'.
enemy_2015_csicem.ability:
Attack power increases greatly when attacking Frozen targets
When <'Snow Doe'> dies, greatly increases ASPD and Movement Speed, and inherits its ability

enemy_1087_ltwolf_2.name: Winterwisp Hound Pro
enemy_1087_ltwolf_2.desc: A pack leader trained and utilized by locals since ancient times. For early aboriginals and pioneers, these hounds were considered invaluable partners.

enemy_1090_mdsnpr_2.name: Mudrock Matterllurgist Leader
enemy_1090_mdsnpr_2.desc: A member of Mudrock's Sarkaz squad. Integrating Sarkaz tactics with Leithanian Arts to greater effect, they are capable of inflicting lethal damage with a block of energy, and prioritize attacking units closest to the contract entrance site.
enemy_1090_mdsnpr_2.ability:
Prioritizes attacking units that are vying for control of a 'Gramophone.'
Attacks 2 targets simultaneously

enemy_1006_shield_3.name: Heavy Defender Lieutenant
enemy_1006_shield_3.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers, more dangerous than a Heavy Defender Leader. Equipped with a full set of heavy armor, they usually appear alongside a large number of troops and are extremely hard to defeat. Proceed with caution.

enemy_2016_csphtm.name: Lucian, 'Blood Diamond'
enemy_2016_csphtm.desc: Calamity of the Crimson Troupe, codename Phantom. Once the rising star of the troupe, the unfinished masterpiece of the troupe leader. Even after his fallout with the troupe and subsequent descent into insanity, he still keeps the name 'Blood Diamond'.
enemy_2016_csphtm.ability:
Has a chance to dodge Physical and Arts attacks when not blocked. When blocked, swiftly moves behind the target and leaves an  at the original location
Can use skills to deal Physical damage to the surrounding area; attacks and skills ignore a certain amount of DEF and inflict Nervous Impairment

enemy_1099_nbkght_2.name: Nameless Elite Knight
enemy_1099_nbkght_2.desc: An independent Kazimierz knight with some degree of achievement, though not enough to inspire any confidence. Sooner or later, they'll probably get swallowed up by the competition.

enemy_1091_mdhmmr_2.name: Mudrock Zealot Leader
enemy_1091_mdhmmr_2.desc: An elite of Mudrock's Sarkaz squad. Dancing on the edge of life and death has led them to develop a unique tactical system. Using Arts Resonance, they can generate a Barrier that absorbs Arts damage and grants additional Max HP and ASPD while it persists.
enemy_1091_mdhmmr_2.ability:
Has a Barrier that absorbs Arts damage.
Max HP and ASPD significantly increases while the Barrier persists.

enemy_1060_emouse_2.name: Greytail Leader
enemy_1060_emouse_2.desc: The Rat King's elite guard, granted special Originium Arts by the Rat King, allowing him to create a Barrier to protect himself. Movement speed is increased when the Barrier is broken. Stronger in combat than a Greytail.
enemy_1060_emouse_2.ability:
DEF significantly increases when the Barrier is present.
Movement Speed significantly increases when the Barrier is destroyed.

enemy_1172_dugago_2.name: Enraged Tombkeeper Grotesque
enemy_1172_dugago_2.desc: A special Arts creation from the Dublinn forces, created by their leader Mandragora. According to Mandragora, back when she was studying Originium Arts in an outskirts cemetery, the Tombkeeper Grotesque was her first companion. Its outer appearance has gone through many redesigns, with the latest being based off a Sarkaz from an ancient bloodline. This series of Tombkeepers embodies Mandragora's fury towards Victoria.
enemy_1172_dugago_2.ability:
After first defeat, temporarily transforms into a stone statue with extremely high DEF, then revives as an aerial unit
Refraction

enemy_2018_csdoll.name: Big Sad Lock
enemy_2018_csdoll.desc: One of the works of the troupe's host, it isn't very well received amongst the troupe. They do not recognize the stiff manipulation of a puppet as a kind of art, and its rough approach to things is constantly criticized by other members.
enemy_2018_csdoll.ability:
Attacks 2 enemies simultaneously
Has high endurance and powerful recovery abilities, and enemies continuously appear when on the field
Periodically casts a Barrier to protect itself. While the Barrier is active, it does not attack, but all enemies' movement speed increases and additional enemies appear.
After receiving a certain number of hits, deals Arts damage to all allied units
Drains 30 Life Points when a long time has passed in the battle

enemy_2019_cshost.name: 'Troupe Mouthpiece'
enemy_2019_cshost.desc: The host of the Grand Drama, its mind is completely taken up by its dazzling Arts. The moment it abandoned the Witch King, its curse was deeply etched into the betrayer's spirit. Even so, who cares?
enemy_2019_cshost.ability:
Permanently Invulnerable
Attacks 2 enemies simultaneously and inflicts extra Nervous Impairment
Controls  in combat.  reduce the ATK of and deal continuous True damage to nearby allied units
Loses HP whenever a  is killed

enemy_1086_ltsodr_2.name: Leithanian Rebel Leader
enemy_1086_ltsodr_2.desc: One of Leithanien's local militia leaders. The widespread popularity of classical Originium Arts has made them more threatening, and their attacks inflict Arts damage.

enemy_1090_mdsnpr.name: Mudrock Matterllurgist
enemy_1090_mdsnpr.desc: A member of Mudrock's Sarkaz squad. Integrating Sarkaz tactics with Leithanian Arts, they are capable of inflicting lethal damage with a block of energy, and prioritize attacking units closest to the contract entrance site.
enemy_1090_mdsnpr.ability: Prioritizes attacking units that are vying for control of a 'Gramophone.'

enemy_2020_cswrtr.name: 'Playwright'
enemy_2020_cswrtr.desc: A nameless youth toiling behind the scenes, he has never stopped writing, even when he lacks pen and paper. Someone here is worthy of his interest, and he doesn't seem hostile.
enemy_2020_cswrtr.ability:
Deals area damage to Operators on the four cardinal tiles next to the primary target.
Periodically attacks all allied units on the field
Periodically uses Arts to greatly reduce an allied unit's ASPD and deal Arts damage to that unit every second. If Operators under this effect leave the field, the tile they are on will be permanently sealed and cannot be deployed on
When HP drops below half, gains greatly increased DEF and RES, and uses skills more often

enemy_1089_ltlntc.name: Winterwisp Blood Shaman
enemy_1089_ltlntc.desc: Locals who have studied the ancient Arts of Winterwisp are much more terrifying than modern Casters. Their ancient sacrificial rituals are capable of instantly dealing area Arts damage, as well as severing Arts ley lines to seize control of L-44 'Gramophones.'
enemy_1089_ltlntc.ability:
Will gradually lose HP over time
Upon death, deals Arts damage to all surrounding units and immediately seizes control of any 'Gramophones'

enemy_1089_ltlntc_2.name: Winterwisp Blood Magister
enemy_1089_ltlntc_2.desc: A mixed-blood well-versed in Winterwisp's ancient Blood Arts, much more terrifying than modern Casters. Their ancient sacrificial rituals are capable of instantly dealing area Arts damage, as well as severing Arts ley lines to seize control of L-44 'Gramophones.'
enemy_1089_ltlntc_2.ability:
Will gradually lose HP over time
Upon death, deals a large amount of Arts damage to all surrounding units and immediately seizes control of any 'Gramophones'

enemy_1134_diamd_2.name: Originiutant α
enemy_1134_diamd_2.desc: An Originiutant mutant that was weaponized for some purpose. Even more ferocious and terrifying than usual.

enemy_1047_sagent_2.name: Sniper Leader
enemy_1047_sagent_2.desc: A basic ranged soldier. They are stronger than normal Snipers, firing improved ammunition that stuns for a longer duration. Proceed with caution.
enemy_1047_sagent_2.ability: After two attacks, the next attack Stuns its target

enemy_1047_sagent.name: Sniper
enemy_1047_sagent.desc: A basic ranged soldier. They have very long range. Their sniper rifles can launch special ammunition capable of stunning our Operators.
enemy_1047_sagent.ability: After two attacks, the next attack Stuns its target

enemy_1131_sbeast.name: Mutant Sand Beast
enemy_1131_sbeast.desc: A sand beast infected by an Originiutant. Sand beasts are a species of small-sized omnivores that primarily inhabit deserts and hamadas. They are nocturnal in nature and are often tamed by the local Sargonians to be used as hunting beasts.

enemy_1137_plasm.name: Originiutant Excrescence
enemy_1137_plasm.desc: A twisted organism born under the combined effects of radiation mutation and Originium infection.
enemy_1137_plasm.ability: Drains 1 Life Point when entering the Objective Point

enemy_1101_plkght_3.name: 'Plastic' Szewczyk
enemy_1101_plkght_3.desc: The first member of Roar Knightclub's Majors team, now representative of Roar Arena: Tough armor, defensive counterattack, a hundred thousand fans, worth a million! Don't forget! 'Roar Guards, even the winds will bow to you!'
enemy_1101_plkght_3.ability: Starts with significantly increased DEF and RES, persists for a long period.

enemy_1103_wdkght_3.name: 'Brassrust' Olmer Ingra
enemy_1103_wdkght_3.desc: The great knight known as the 'Butcher of the Field'! Convicted several times for acts of brutality, avoided trial several times thanks to corporate contributions! Youngest son of the Ingra family, an ancient knightly lineage! A ruthless fighter who gets stronger with each battle! Olmerrrrrr Ingraaaaaaa!
enemy_1103_wdkght_3.ability: Each time an enemy is defeated, gains ATK and ASPD, stacking up to 10 times.

enemy_1104_lfkght_3.name: 'Left-hand' Tytus Topola
enemy_1104_lfkght_3.desc: His nose is high as the sun, and he's almost as terrifyingly strong, if much, much colder. And look how many fans have lined up for him! He's 'Left-hand' Tytus Topola! He weakens his enemies' resolve, crushes their spirit, sneering down at them as they fall to their knees in surrender. That's a Left-handed victory!
enemy_1104_lfkght_3.ability:
Significantly decreases ATK of the unit with the highest ATK for a period of time
Rebirth

enemy_1184_cadkgt_3.name: The Candle Knight Viviana
enemy_1184_cadkgt_3.desc: The Candle Knight, Viviana Droste. Words fail to describe her beauty, her brilliant eyes alone are enchanting enough. Tonight, the marigolds bloom for her.
enemy_1184_cadkgt_3.ability:
[Glimmering Touch] After charging for some time, deals a large amount of Arts damage to a target and all surrounding units
While charging, loses Invisibility

enemy_1059_buster_2.name: Desperate Fanatic
enemy_1059_buster_2.desc: Special long-range combatants, with greater combat capabilities than normal Fanatics. Wielding a massive iron hammer, they boast enormous destructive potential and will targeted attacking facilities first. They can also hit Operators stationed on high ground.

enemy_1010_demon_2.name: Sarkaz Greatswordsman Leader
enemy_1010_demon_2.desc: A mercenary from Sarkaz, more dangerous than a normal Sarkaz Swordsman. The Originium inside them has boosted their physical abilities, allowing them to wield larger weapons with ease and withstand more damage.

enemy_1516_jakill.name: Jesselton Williams
enemy_1516_jakill.desc: Jesselton Williams, a killer who infiltrated the prison disguised as a jailer. Very particular in both speech and action, he remains graceful even in the midst of battle.
enemy_1516_jakill.ability:
Jailer Form
RES is greatly increased
Attacks become ranged and deal Arts damage, and no longer attacks Imprisonment Devices
Can Stun multiple targets
The first time HP drops to 0, enters Killer Form
Killer Form
Frees all imprisoned enemies when entering Killer Form
Gains significantly increased DEF
Uses melee Physical attacks that ignore a certain amount of the target's DEF

enemy_1059_buster.name: Fanatic
enemy_1059_buster.desc: Special long-range combatants. Wielding a massive iron hammer, they boast enormous destructive potential and will targeted attacking facilities first. They can also hit Operators stationed on high ground.

enemy_1121_lifbos.name: Recidivist
enemy_1121_lifbos.desc: A vicious inmate feared by inmates and guards alike. Has learned patience after his long stay in prison, and pays no attention to normal attacks and provocations. However, avoid enraging him at all costs, or be prepared to face the consequences.
enemy_1121_lifbos.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced but gains increased DEF
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK
When freed for the first time, immediately frees all other enemies

enemy_1120_vofwiz.name: Caster Prisoner
enemy_1120_vofwiz.desc: An inmate with a weak constitution. Due to being unable to overcome his bindings on his own, he's instead learned to utilize defensive techniques using the devices on his body. On the other hand, he's well-versed in Originium Arts and will become very difficult to deal with should he be freed.
enemy_1120_vofwiz.ability:
All enemies gain increased ASPD when on the field
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, does not attack but gains increased DEF
After being freed, attacks 2 enemies simultaneously and prioritizes attacking Imprisonment Devices

relics.name: Hot Water Kettle
relics.desc: Some people often use the hot water kettle in the Rhodes Island Office to cook instant noodles in the middle of the night. That's not a very healthy lifestyle...
relics.usage: Immediately gain +2 Life Points and +1 Hope

relics.name: Special Suppressor
relics.desc: An enhanced suppressor developed by Rhodes Island that can better keep the infected from losing control of their Originium Arts.
relics.usage: Immediately gain +6 Life Points.

relics.name: Stone Gargoyle
relics.desc: A palm-sized sculpture. Supposedly they are handmade gifts given out by the Gargoyles as a sign of friendship.
relics.usage: Immediately gain +6 Life Points.

relics.name: Vampire's Bed
relics.desc: A lavish-looking coffin that matches the castle's aesthetics well. However, Warfarin says this is merely a stereotype.
relics.usage: Immediately gain +8 Life Points.

relics.name: Proof of Longevity
relics.desc:
A tree branch that has withered and flourished. 
When it is withered, it grows anew. When it is thriving in its fullest, it fades away yet again.
relics.usage: Immediately gain +10 Life Points.

relics.name: Pried Open Toolbox
relics.desc: The tool box of the troupe's prop master. From nails to dummy props, it has everything.
relics.usage: Hope +4

relics.name: Antiquated Sheet Music
relics.desc: A tattered piece of sheet music. The notes can no longer be clearly read. No one knows for certain what story this piece was meant to tell.
relics.usage: Hope +4

relics.name: Letter of Termination Contract
relics.desc: You can break free of this troupe with just one autograph.
relics.usage: Hope +4

relics.name: Universal Key
relics.desc: An old, silver key. It can open any door. As the keeper, the troupe's butler naturally needs a key that unlocks all the secrets here.
relics.usage: Hope +6

relics.name: Banshee's Kiss
relics.desc: Outsiders make extraordinary displays of bravery that far surpass their normal abilities. However, as one of the few male Banshees out there, this has become a far too common sight to Logos.
relics.usage: Hope +8

relics.name: Antique Coins
relics.desc: An ancient, rusty coin, weathered to the point where one can no longer identify its age. Things like these are all too common throughout Terra's barrens.
relics.usage: Immediately adds +10 Originium Ingots.

relics.name: Actor's Jewelry Box
relics.desc: A box containing all kinds of shattered jewelries. The young lead actress wore these jewelries and stepped onto the stage, in the end falling from its highest point.
relics.usage: Immediately adds +18 Originium Ingots.

relics.name: Flawless Jadestone
relics.desc: Even though it thrice brought the city to ruins, it yet shines radiantly in its flawless form. Even today, the Sargon emperor in possession of the gem has yet to find another piece of jewelry matching its brilliance, leaving it with naught but gunsmoke for a companion.
relics.usage: Immediately adds +25 Originium Ingots.

relics.name: Laughing Joker
relics.desc: You are granted this privilege.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote an Operator (Does not require Hope).

relics.name: Miss.Christine Petting Ticket
relics.desc: You have the lady's permission!
relics.usage: Immediately Promote 2 Operators (Does not require Hope).

relics.name: Antique Casting
relics.desc: 'Harken the forge of the sky Awaits five earthborn metals' ...Even though it can be used for all kinds of work, nobody understands its exact mechanism.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote 3 Operators (Does not require Hope).

relics.name: Blunt Claws - Training
relics.desc: A formidable set of equipment to provide strength, an indomitable heart to press forward bravely.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Vanguard Operator (Does not require Hope).

relics.name: Bend Spears - Training
relics.desc: Some people use swords because they don't know how to use crossbows or Arts. Others use swords because that's all they need.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Guard Operator (Does not require Hope).

relics.name: Iron Guard - Training
relics.desc: Scars symbolize glory, but who will remember the soldiers who were cut a bit too deeply?
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Defender Operator (Does not require Hope).

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Training
relics.desc: 'Either you learn to control your ignition, or you learn to discern your target. Got it?'
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Sniper Operator (Does not require Hope).

relics.name: Broken Wand - Training
relics.desc: 'After watching the 'performance' of certain casters, I decided to quietly put away the Leithanian textbook.'
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Caster Operator (Does not require Hope).

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Training
relics.desc: Their tactics are always diverse and colorful.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Supporter Operator (Does not require Hope).

relics.name: Healer's Path - Training
relics.desc: When the day comes when everyone is a doctor and can even cure the disease known as life, we will no longer need doctors.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Medic Operator (Does not require Hope).

relics.name: Rusted Blade - Training
relics.desc: When conducting live combat drills with Specialist Operators, please be prepared to be 'beaten up thoroughly before you are prepared.'
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Specialist Operator (Does not require Hope).

relics.name: Regional Action Plan
relics.desc: Rhodes Island has established dedicated offices in every important hub area. The messengers are doing what they can to weave a fragile web across this land.
relics.usage: 4-star Operators cost -2 Hope to recruit.

relics.name: Comprehensive Operation File
relics.desc: A vessel sailing upon the land, like treading upon thin ice. Remember to always expand your worldview.
relics.usage: 5-star Operators cost -2 Hope to recruit.

relics.name: Secret HR Letter
relics.desc: '■■■ gave this to you? Guess there's no problem then.'
relics.usage: 6-star Operators cost -2 Hope to recruit

relics.name: Draft of a Speech
relics.desc: She was a little nervous, a little worried, but still remained resolute. When the microphone was turned on, her long ears quivered slightly.

relics.desc: Esteemed guests, welcome to the Crimson Troupe. The performance we have in store for you tonight will be one to remember.

relics.name: 'Silent Squad'

relics.usage: All enemy units have -12% ATK.

relics.name: Worn-out Group Photo

relics.usage: All enemy units have -20% HP.

relics.usage: All friendly units have +15% DEF.

relics.name: Necklace of the Presence

relics.name: 'Brilliant Lament'
relics.desc: His blood yet flows. He has never left this place.

relics.usage: All friendly units have +30% healing effectiveness

relics.desc: Troupe actors put this on before going onstage. Stable yet aromatic fragrances like this can help them ease pressure. That's how it seems on the surface, at least.

It's very bright outside, but it's hardly usable in here.

He's wearing the mask.
