relics.name: Iron Guard - Advancement
relics.desc: The shieldbearers formed a mountain range and became the earth. What they fight against are not enemies of flesh and blood. They fight against fate and injustice.
relics.usage: Defender Operators have -3 DP Cost and +40% Max HP

relics.name: Iron Guard - Invasion
relics.desc: Defensive actions for the sake of teammates or strategic purposes often make people forget about their original aggressiveness.
relics.usage: Defender Operators have +60% ATK.

relics.name: Iron Guard - Tranquility
relics.desc: 'Is there anyone who can remain in formation under a bombing of that scale?' 'Standing in formation? He moved a few steps forward.'
relics.usage: Defender Operators have +25% DEF and +50% Max HP

relics.name: Iron Guard - Advance
relics.desc: In exchange for an opportunity to attack, the shattered ranks made one more advance.
relics.usage: Defender Operators have -1 Block (will not be reduced below 1), but gain +40% ATK and +40 ASPD.

relics.name: Iron Guard - Impenetrable
relics.desc: Every blow they endure will turn into anger and be returned severalfold.
relics.usage: Defender Operators restore 2 SP after being attacked.

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Advancement
relics.desc: Load, aim, and fire. Destruction arrives as scheduled.
relics.usage: Sniper Operators have -2 DP Cost and +60% Max HP

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Precision
relics.desc: In an era when Originium Arts were not as robust as they are now, Yan had a saying, 'a pierced willow leaf from a hundred paces,' to praise others for their mastery of archery.
relics.usage: Sniper Operators have +20% ATK.

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Synergy
relics.desc: The more intimate a sniper is with her weapon, the less likely she is to be betrayed by it.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Sniper Operators by +0.5/s

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Crossfire
relics.desc: The dense crossfire also exposes snipers to great danger. The next battle boils down to a game of vision.
relics.usage: Sniper Operators have -40% HP, but gain +40% ATK.

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Divine Speed
relics.desc: It is said that the legendary archer of ancient Victoria can almost shoot a continuous stream of arrows.
relics.usage: Sniper Operators have +70 ASPD.

relics.name: Broken Wand - Advancement
relics.desc: Let Arts dance around your fingertips. Call the wind and rain, unravel reality, and achieve your grand goals.
relics.usage: Caster Operators have -3 DP Cost and +60% Max HP

relics.name: Broken Wand - Arts Weaving
relics.desc: Those who gaze up at the great spires in Leithanien often see some strange or even celestial phenomena.
relics.usage: Caster Operators have +25% ATK.

relics.name: Broken Wand - Chanting
relics.desc: Though the relationship between music and Arts is marvelous indeed, umm, this is not an excuse for some peoples' tone-deafness.
relics.usage: Caster Operators have +40 ASPD.

relics.name: Broken Wand - Concentration
relics.desc: 'When a caster intends to go all out, they will display a completely different temperament — even if we're talking about something like Miss Skyfire's grumpiness.'
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Caster Operators by +0.4/s

relics.name: Broken Wand - Malediction
relics.desc: It is virtually impossible to identify when the Sarkaz first encountered Originium, and the origins of these ancient Arts have long been separated from actual reality and common sense.
relics.usage: Caster Operators have -40% HP, but deal +70% Arts damage

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Advancement
relics.desc: Whenever you're 'almost done' with a task, what you need at that moment is someone to help you with the 'almost' part.
relics.usage: Supporter Operators have -2 DP Cost and +60% Max HP

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Secondary Front
relics.desc: 'Um, I can understand the derivatives of combining mechanics with Originium Arts, but there might be something else...'
relics.usage: Units summoned by Supporter Operators have +50% ATK.

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Diligence
relics.desc: Operators tend to give it their all, even to tasks that might seem trivial. The reality is, those tasks are not trivial at all.
relics.usage: The SP Cost of Supporter Operators' skills is decreased by -40%.

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Demoralize
relics.desc: There are many ways to weaken an enemy's offensive capabilities: corroding their weapons, psychological warfare, increasing their weight, and... giving them a stomachache.
relics.usage: Enemies within the attack range of Supporter Operators have -15% ATK.

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Counter-Arts
relics.desc: There are many ways to weaken an enemy's defensive capabilities: positional devices, armor-stripping Arts, marking weak points, and... giving them a stomachache.
relics.usage: Enemies within the attack range of Supporter Operators have -20% DEF and -20% RES.

relics.name: Healer's Path - Advancement
relics.desc: The desire to save lives is a virtue that mankind must applaud, for that is the support that keeps us alive.
relics.usage: Medic Operators have -2 DP Cost and +60% Max HP

relics.name: Healer's Path - Self-Treating
relics.desc: Who says healers can't heal themselves? They simply pop some pills before continuing to help others.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Medic Operators by +0.3/s

relics.name: Healer's Path - Potency
relics.desc: Nobody else dared to attempt such a radical treatment — except Warfarin. She never made a single mistake.
relics.usage: Medic Operators have +40% ATK.

relics.name: Healer's Path - Keen Hands
relics.desc: Compared to the feat of snatching a life from the grip of death, what is three days of coma afterwards?
relics.usage: Medic Operators have +50 ASPD.

relics.name: Healer's Path - Restore Sanity
relics.desc: Reinforce your thinking. In the domain of your mind, make even every weed impregnable.
relics.usage: Allied units within the attack range of Medic Operators gain Resistance.

relics.name: Rusted Blade - Advancement
relics.desc: There may be many means, but only one end.
relics.usage: Specialist Operators have -2 DP Cost and +60% Max HP

relics.name: Rusted Blade - Execution
relics.desc: Projekt Red has a knife. She has a knife.
relics.usage: Specialist Operators have +30 ASPD.

relics.name: Rusted Blade - Isolation
relics.desc: The director of S.W.E.E.P. does not have any mission participation records. *None*.
relics.usage: Specialist Operators have +40% ATK and DEF

relics.name: Rusted Blade - No Man's Land
relics.desc: Some operators have very peculiar combat methods. Don't make a fuss over how many times you see them, or how many of them you see.
relics.usage: The Redeployment Time of Specialist Operators -35%.

relics.name: Rusted Blade - Overwhelm
relics.desc: Terrain is one aspect of battle... That's all I can say.
relics.usage: Specialist Operators have +2 shift strength.

relics.name: Hand of Spikes
relics.desc: Let the enemy make his move. He will be his own downfall.
relics.usage: Protector, Arts Protector, and Duelist Operators deal Arts damage equal to 100% of their own DEF on enemy units they block

relics.name: Hand of Choker
relics.desc: With your last strength, break open a last bit of hope for yourself.
relics.usage: Heavyshooter, Deadeye, and Besieger Operators' attacks will defeat all non-Boss enemy units at 30% or less HP

relics.name: Hand of Buckler
relics.desc: Drop by drop, bit by bit. It is unstoppable.
relics.usage: Decel Binder, Hexer, and Abjurer Operators' attacks deal extra Arts damage equal to 3% of the target's HP

relics.name: Hand of Diffusion
relics.desc: The more enemies you strike, the more power you take hold of.
relics.usage: Splash Caster, Chain Caster, and Blast Caster Operators recover 2 SP every time they attack an enemy unit

relics.name: Hand of Shredder
relics.desc: Tear it all apart. Their armor is nothing but cloth in your hands.
relics.usage: Dreadnought, Swordmaster, and Instructor Operators' attacks ignore 70% of target's DEF

relics.name: Hand of Superspeed
relics.desc: You swing your hand, leaving not even an afterimage.
relics.usage: Executor, Ambusher, and Dollkeeper Operators gain +100 ASPD when there are no friendly units in the eight adjacent tiles

relics.name: Hand of Snatcher
relics.desc: Whatever it is you do not have, snatch it away from your enemy. There is no need to hesitate.
relics.usage: Charger, Pioneer, and Tactician Operators' DP Cost -12 and gain 6 additional DP after defeating an enemy

relics.name: Blue Silk Scarf
relics.desc: A lady's elegant scarf. Her appearance is a sign that it is safe here.
relics.usage: Hope +2, Squad Size Limit +1

relics.name: Red Bow Tie
relics.desc: The first present Phantom gave Miss.Christine. She is really fond of it.
relics.usage: Hope +2, Deployment Limit +1

relics.name: Weird Flute
relics.desc: A weird-looking flute. Someone plays it day in and day out.
relics.usage: Hope +4, Squad Size Limit +1

relics.name: Glass Bird
relics.desc: 'In this paradise of mine, the bird will sing forever and ever.'
relics.usage: Hope +4, Squad Size Limit +1

relics.name: Solo Music Box
relics.desc: An old music box. The music does not play when the box is opened, nor does the figure inside show any signs it is about to move. There is not a peep, and no one is breaking out in dance. Before she begins her performances with Phantom, the lead actress stares blankly into the music box.
relics.usage: Hope +4, Deployment Limit +1

relics.name: Originium Iris
relics.desc: A most extravagant bouquet of flowers. The troupe leader has handed flowers like these to the leads during countless curtain calls.
relics.usage: Hope +6, Squad Size Limit +2

relics.name: Pure Gold Expedition
relics.desc: The regent departed from the wastelands, and markets rose from beneath her dress like sprouts budding in the spring. The road of prosperity connected high mountains and dense forests, following her until she disappeared beyond the cliffs overlooking the western waves. —Ancient Sargon Fairy Tale
relics.usage: Hope +4, Squad Size Limit +1, Deployment Limit +1

relics.name: 'Durin Overground'
relics.desc: In this expedition account, we get a glimpse of how those who live underground see those who live on the surface.
relics.usage: Gain 1 Temporary Life Point at the beginning of each battle.

relics.name: 'Gaulish Toponym Origins'
relics.desc: A book of historical place names that Mint dug out of the Doctor's office. Calais-Blason is mentioned in the book.
relics.usage: Gain 2 Temporary Life Points at the beginning of each battle.

relics.name: Ancient Gaulish Silver Coin
relics.desc: A coin once circulated in the now fallen nation 'Gaul.' It is in very good shape, making it highly valuable as a collector's item.
relics.usage: All Operators start with +6 SP.

relics.name: Élysée Purse
relics.desc: A purse used by high-class ladies who shop on the Élysée. Mousse has a purse like this.
relics.usage: All Operators start with +8 SP.

relics.name: Bank of Gaul Check
relics.desc: An extravagant checkbook plated in gold and silver. Can no longer be cashed.
relics.usage: All Operators start with +12 SP.

relics.name: 'Second Economic Reform Act'
relics.desc: With this bill as the starting point, Gaul formally began its path to become a hegemonic empire.
relics.usage: All Operators start with +18 SP.

relics.name: Vanilla Soda
relics.desc: Bolívar's cheap specialty drink dates back hundreds of years. It is mixed with a variety of spices, and is known for not being very good.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Auto Recovery skills by +0.2/s

relics.name: Ball Juice
relics.desc: They call it fruit juice, but it's actually alcohol. The bottle itself states that minors are forbidden from drinking it. Its Victorian place of origin once fell within Gaul's territory.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Auto Recovery skills by +0.25/s

relics.name: Fowlbeast Liver Pate
relics.desc: A small jar of fowlbeast liver pâté. Bright and vividly colorful, it is eaten only by nobles.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Auto Recovery skills by +0.35/s

relics.name: Dreaming Essence
relics.desc: Supposedly the Banshees make their aromatics only for their beloved. A single drop is enough to enchant just about anyone, bringing out any artist's inspiration and creativity.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Auto Recovery skills by +0.5/s

relics.name: Barrens Tequila
relics.desc: A cheap distilled spirit originally produced in Bolívar. It has a strong taste and is widely praised by Columbia's explorers.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Offensive and Defensive Recovery skills by 1 SP per 3.5s.

relics.name: Captain Morgan's Wine
relics.desc: A wine produced in the Iberian region. Despite having gained popularity in the heart of Victoria's bustling cities, it is now discontinued.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Offensive and Defensive Recovery skills by 1 SP per 3s.

relics.name: Water of Life
relics.desc: The strongest Ursine liquors go straight to one's head, but it's said that the healthy Ursine adult can down several bottles.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Offensive and Defensive Recovery skills by 1 SP per 2.5s.

relics.name: Royal Liqueur
relics.desc: The fine wines of the extinct country, Gaul, have now become expensive, prime treasures for collectors. The place of production is now a part of Leithanien, but the technique has been lost.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Offensive and Defensive Recovery skills by 1 SP per 1.5s.

relics.name: 'Unleashings'
relics.desc: After several attempts by Touch, nature briefly opens up to you.
relics.usage: All Operators recover 1 SP every time they take damage or Elemental Injury

relics.name: 'Renowned Singer'
relics.desc: Who cares what it's singing? Make it stop! It's hurting my ears!
relics.usage: Enemies receive 600 Arts Damage per second when stunned, frozen, or bound

relics.name: String Puppet
relics.desc: The more you struggle and try to break free, the tighter the strings get. Do you really think you are still thinking rationally?
relics.usage: Enemies receive 700 Arts Damage per second when stunned, frozen, or bound

relics.name: 'Children's Puppet'
relics.desc: A simple puppet made by Iberian children using trinkets found on the seacoast. Supposedly just holding it tight will give you much richer dreams.
relics.usage: Enemies receive 1000 Arts Damage per second when stunned, frozen, or bound

relics.name: Coin-Operated Toy
relics.desc: No idea why anyone would like a design like this, but its overall concept is indeed praiseworthy—Just insert a coin, and even a statue will start moving. There is nothing truer in this world.
relics.usage: For each 5 Originium Ingots in possession, all friendly units have +3 ASPD

relics.name: Chivalric Commandments - New Edition
relics.desc: Taking solemn vows is a thing of the past. The spirit of modern knighthood requires more flexible displays. Go ahead and input your number in the communications device, and you too will be an honorary knight.
relics.usage: For each 5 Originium Ingots in possession, all friendly units have +5 ASPD

relics.name: Golden Chalice
relics.desc: Saints, knights, and pietists. All of them are madmen. Dear God, even the chalice itself is made of pure gold!
relics.usage: For each 5 Originium Ingots in possession, all friendly units have +7 ASPD

relics.name: Spinach Pack
relics.desc: Many Cautus out there believe that popping the contents of this can into their mouths will grant them immense strength. The marketing's effectiveness is a classic success story in the industry.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +60% ATK for one second after their skill becomes active

relics.name: No. 073 Safety Reagent
relics.desc: A perfectly safe product of a cautiously controlled experiment. Perfectly safe, definitely will not explode, and keeps your mind at ease.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +65% ATK for one second after their skill becomes active

relics.name: Wrath of Siracusans
relics.desc: The signature pasta dish of an 'authentic Siracusan restaurant' that once operated in the capital of Gaul. The dish's unique taste, and particularly its sauce, a blend of thick chocolate sauce and Originium Slug liver, earned the praise of many Gaulish nobles. Supposedly, though, this dish nearly caused a diplomatic crisis between Siracusa and Gaul.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +100% ATK for one second after their skill becomes active

relics.name: 'Civilight Eterna'
relics.desc: A small, black crown.
relics.usage: All enemies take +150% True damage.

relics.name: Ribbon of Honor
relics.desc: Numerous actors have played this character, yet none of them were able to save the character's dearest friend.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +100% ATK when blocking only one enemy

relics.name: 'Loyality'
relics.desc: What is loyalty, and what is righteousness? A group of people found the answer several decades ago.
relics.usage: All friendly units recover 150 HP per second when blocking three or more enemies

relics.name: 'End of Times'
relics.desc: As long as a new cycle has not been reached, there is still time to fix your mistakes.
relics.usage: For one time only during any battle prior to the final battle of the area, gain 1 Life Point and continue onto the next step instead of concluding the exploration upon mission failure

relics.name: Royal Alliance Treaty
relics.desc: The old Sarkaz courts sacrificed themselves to obtain their power. Ever since, the courts have come to terms with one another, banding together against their common enemy.
relics.usage: All friendly units no longer receive any damage from Sarkaz enemies

relics.name: Condensed Suppressant
relics.desc: A medical reagent developed by Rhodes Island to suppress acute Oripathy infections. However, further enrichment of the reagent's concentration will cause irreversible damage to the infection site.
relics.usage: All friendly units increase their ATK to 150% and silence the enemy for 3 seconds when attacking [Infected Creatures]

relics.name: Ursus Chachek
relics.desc: The currents of steel and iron gush forth on the barren plains. From afar surge an inviolable, absolute will. Wheresoever my hands reach, there stands Ursus.
relics.usage: All enemy units have +40% ATK, DEF, and Max HP

relics.name: Ursus Chachek (Reforged)
relics.desc: The currents of steel and iron gush forth on the barren plains. From afar surge an inviolable, absolute will. Wheresoever my hands reach, there stands Ursus.
relics.usage: All enemy units gain +35% ATK, DEF, and Max HP, and gain an additional +10% to these three stats upon entering a new floor, but clearing an Emergency Op reduces these stats by 5% (minimum 35%)

relics.name: Victoria Crown
relics.desc: When the last Aslan to rule Victoria was sent to the guillotine, the angry masses rushed forward to trample his reign into dust.
relics.usage: All enemy units have +30% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Boss units gain an additional +15% ATK, +15% DEF, and +30% Max HP

relics.name: Victoria Crown (Reforged)
relics.desc: When the last Aslan to rule Victoria was sent to the guillotine, the angry masses rushed forward to trample his reign into dust.
relics.usage: All enemy units have +35% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Leader enemies gain an additional +20% ATK, DEF and +50% Max HP, but these leader bonuses are halved when your squad has Operators from all 8 Classes

relics.name: Leithanien Sceptre
relics.desc: It was not long ago that the Witch King's scepter flowed with daylight and the night sky.
relics.usage: All enemy units have +30% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Entering a new node decreases Life Point by 1 (cannot be reduced beneath 1 this way)

relics.name: Leithanien Sceptre (Reforged)
relics.desc: It was not long ago that the Witch King's scepter flowed with daylight and the night sky.
relics.usage: All enemy units have +35% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Entering a new node decreases Life Point by 1 (cannot be reduced beneath 1 this way); When Life Point is below 3, all units have DP Cost + 2 and SP recovery rate -20%

relics.name: Gaul Mantle
relics.desc: An artifact left behind by the late Gaulish emperor. A symbol of power and glory.
relics.usage: All enemy units have +25% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; 4-star and above Operators cost +1 Hope when recruited

relics.name: Gaul Mantle (Reforged)
relics.desc: An artifact left behind by the late Gaulish emperor. A symbol of power and glory.
relics.usage: All enemy units have +30% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; 4-star and above Operators cost +2 Hope to recruit on odd-numbered floors, and +2 Hope to promote on even-numbered floors

relics.name: Half-Refined Diamond
relics.desc: Half of it shines radiantly in the name of art, while the other half is stained in a madder red color that signifies madness. This is the 'Phantom' that the troupe leader has always dreamt of.
relics.usage: All enemy units have +25% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Gain -50% Command EXP from battles

relics.name: Half-Refined Diamond (Reforged)
relics.desc: Half of it shines radiantly in the name of art, while the other half is stained in a madder red color that signifies madness. This is the 'Phantom' that the troupe leader has always dreamt of.
relics.usage: All enemy units have +30% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Gain -70% Command EXP from battles; Dreadful Foe nodes grant 15x Command EXP

relics.name: Sigil of Tragodia
relics.desc: A symbol that can be seen everywhere within the Crimson Troupe. It burns the troupe leader and his pursuit for the arts into the eyes of all those who lay their eyes upon it.
relics.usage: All enemy units have +30% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Deployment Limit -2

relics.name: Sigil of Tragodia (Reforged)
relics.desc: A symbol that can be seen everywhere within the Crimson Troupe. It burns the troupe leader and his pursuit for the arts into the eyes of all those who lay their eyes upon it.
relics.usage: All enemy units have +35% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Deployment Limit -3, but gain Deployment Limit +2 whenever you enter the Downtime Recreation node

relics.name: Playwright's Manuscript: Victoria
relics.desc: 'The Feline's crown shatters into pieces—'
relics.usage: All enemy units have +10% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Operators cost +1 Hope to recruit (effect lasts for 1 recruit)

relics.name: Playwright's Manuscript: Ursus
relics.desc: 'The Ursus's feast comes to an abrupt stop—'
relics.usage: All enemy units have +10% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; Lose 1 Originium Ingot every time you enter a non-combat node

relics.name: Playwright's Manuscript: Leithanien
relics.desc: 'The Caprinae's spire collapses—'
relics.usage: All enemy units have +10% ATK, DEF, and Max HP; All allied units have -10% ATK when DP is below 99

relics.name: Playwright's Manuscript: Gaul
relics.desc: 'The Liberi's bricks turn into dust—'
relics.usage: All enemy units have +10% ATK, DEF, and Max HP and the bonus is doubled when you lose Life Points for the first time in each battle

relics.name: 'Right Eye of the Natator'
relics.desc: One half of the Gaulish empress's crown is now housed in the Royal Museum of Londinium. When she learned of the death of the emperor, the empress consort was determined to fight her enemies to the end—for her country, and for her departed beloved.
relics.usage: Guard and Sniper Operators have +15% ATK, but Supporter and Caster Operators have -5% ATK

relics.name: 'Left Eye of the Natator'
relics.desc: One half of the Gaulish empress's crown is now stored housed in the Leithanian Queen Library. The empress dismissed all her attendants, granted clemency to all criminals, and sat solemnly alone on the throne that was once her husband's, awaiting the invaders—She was determined to die not by at hands of her enemies, but under the effects of Originium crystals.
relics.usage: Supporter and Caster Operators have +15% ATK, but Guard and Sniper Operators have -5% ATK

relics.name: 'Magnificent Visage'
relics.desc: It's a good thing that a glimpse of her face feels you with hope—and especially so if you knew of the disaster.
relics.usage: Gain +1 Hope upon entering a noncombat node.

relics.name: 'Blademace'
relics.desc: A sword lodged in a stone. Was someone bored enough to do this?
relics.usage: All Operators have +5 DP Cost, but gain +10% ATK, DEF, and Max HP

relics.name: 'Brokenblade'
relics.desc: A broken sword. It's usable... probably...
relics.usage: All Operators have -30% Max HP, but have -50% Redeployment Time

relics.name: Familiar Sculpture
relics.desc: A familiar face but a different vibe. Perhaps you have met this person?
relics.usage: All enemy units have -15 ASPD.

relics.name: Proof of Friendship
relics.desc: Some old Vampire families are still concerned with formalities and etiquette. When they extend to others their exalted friendship, they do not allow it to be sullied.
relics.usage: +30% Originium Ingot drops from battle.

relics.name: Jet-Black Dance Shoes
relics.desc: The Columbian dancer's steps are firm and strong, her taps reverberating loudly in the air as she strikes the ground.
relics.usage: Can deploy Melee Operators on High Ground tiles

relics.name: Pure White Dance Shoes
relics.desc: The Ursus dancer's steps are swift and elegant, her body moving as she wills.
relics.usage: Can deploy Ranged Operators on Low Ground tiles

relics.name: 'Bladedance'
relics.desc: An artifact left behind by a 'Crimson Troupe' member. 'Bladedance' was a quiet Sargon dancer as well as a warrior of spectacular martial prowess, and her loyalty to the troupe is unparalleled. Even among the members of the troupe, there are few people who know of her dark past.
relics.usage: When the battle begins, a random Operator will have +100% ATK and +100% Max HP within the operation

relics.name: 'Whiteflower'
relics.desc: An artifact left behind by a 'Crimson Troupe' member. The stage name 'White Solanum' belonged to a famous Leithanian opera actress. Supposedly, the actress revered as the 'Whiteflower that stands atop the high tower' had complex relationships with many of the tower's nobles, and she even tried to spark a few scuffles among them.
relics.usage: When the battle begins, randomize all allied operators' Deployment Cost

relics.name: 'Shadow'
relics.desc: An artifact left behind by a 'Crimson Troupe' member. The rumor has it that, before he joined the troupe, the 'Shadow' was a Siracusan stage caster famous for his superb illusionary techniques. It is also said that he killed many members of the family during his performances with his Originium Arts.
relics.usage: Increase the probability of Temporary Recruitment when using Recruitment Voucher

relics.name: 'Flowers of Quinde'
relics.desc: An epic narrative poem etched on a stone tablet. It was written eight hundred years ago by a famous Gaulish poet, but although modern literary scholars hold the poem in extremely high regard, historical records show that he was often ridiculed by his contemporaries for his obese figure and unattractive facial features.
relics.usage: All friendly units recover 10 HP every second

relics.name: 'Numbness and Obscenity'
relics.desc: A famous art piece and the final piece created by an insane Leithanian sculptor two hundred years ago. The sculptor was known for his extraordinary talent and his participation in multiple cruel homicides. However, historical records show that few people took notice of his work. He was instead known for his features and beauty.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +5 RES

relics.name: 'Beauty and Ugliness in the Age of Terra'
relics.desc: A legendary book published in Victoria fifteen years ago. The book describes the author's appreciation of aesthetics in simple terms, and it contains a comprehensive record of literary, artistic and musical knowledge and history. The book covers both the past and present and is an easy read, with many honoring it as the 'great art encyclopedia of our generation.' Yet it is rumored that the book's author was the leader of a certain small troupe and was murdered a few years ago by a hitman codenamed 'Blood Diamond.'
relics.usage: For one time only during any battle prior to the final battle of the area, gain 10 Life Points and continue onto the next step instead of concluding the exploration upon mission failure

relics.name: Worn-out Puppet
relics.desc: 'He said he has had enough. He will be a puppet no longer.'
relics.usage: The adventure will head towards a different ending

relics.name: Useless Scissors
relics.desc: This pair of scissors has been used for far, far too long. So long that it can no longer cut anything tangible, and so long that it can cut all things intangible to shreds.
relics.usage: Immediately adds +11 Originium Ingots and +3 Hope

relics.name: Blank Suicide Note
relics.desc: His only wish was to escape the madman who wanted control over him. Now, he got his wish.
relics.usage: Neutralizes Big Sad Lock's Self-Healing abilities

relics.name: Mantle of the Wrongly Condemned
relics.desc: The crazed Caster of the tower organized a special banquet. All the guests who were present at the reception hall out of courtesy became witnesses when the curtain rose.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +2 DP Cost.

relics.name: Stand-in Actor
relics.desc: The playwright never approved of his inauguration of these shows. No matter, though. The master of ceremonies is not discouraged. He has as many understudies at his disposal.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +3% ATK, DEF, and Max HP

relics.name: Abrupt Realization
relics.desc: The Witch King's followers have given everything for the arts. Their flesh, their blood, their beliefs, and their souls.
relics.usage: All friendly units have -3 DP Cost and gain +5% ATK, DEF, and Max HP. The adventure will head towards a different ending

relics.name: Dance of the Condemned
relics.desc: They have trapped themselves in this place. Ah, peace and quiet. No one can interrupt this creation now.
relics.usage: Operator redeployment time -10%

relics.name: Act 1
relics.desc: The end of the first act, the first ending that the young man wrote for himself. In the midst of the fire that consumed all that he treasured, the hero sips his foe's blood and writes the last scene of his vendetta.
relics.usage: Hope +3, leads the exploration toward a different conclusion

relics.name: Today's Menu
relics.desc: The troupe's chef never serves the same dish twice. The members of the troupe have been warned to never ask where the ingredients came from.
relics.usage: All allied units have +40% healing effectiveness

relics.name: 'Intoxicated Hymnoi'
relics.desc: One sniff brings tears to Pallas's eyes. What spell has Lena cast on this perfume?
relics.usage: All allied units recover 2% of Max HP per second

relics.name: Empresses' Wish
relics.desc: The Twin Empresses tower above. When dawn and dusk are one, there shall be no more dissent in Leithanien.
relics.usage: All allied units have +15% Physical and Arts Dodge

relics.name: Victory Horn
relics.desc: Blow the horn, cry out, and scatter our foes with its roar!
relics.usage: Duration of single instance status effects such as stun, cold or freeze is increased by 100%

relics.name: Flash Camera
relics.desc: Every photo shot by this camera is washed out in white, with only the victim's twisted expression faintly visible.
relics.usage: Duration of single instance status effects such as stun, cold or freeze is increased by 110%

relics.name: Therapy Tape
relics.desc: He tried to stop you from listening. He said it was dangerous. He said you should not stay. Do you understand now? You are listening to his song.
relics.usage: Duration of single instance status effects such as stun, cold or freeze is increased by 150%

relics.name: Royal Brooch
relics.desc: An ornament worn by Gaulish royalty, made from gold and inlaid with expensive Originium crystals. They are more than decoration: many of these brooches hide Originium circuits loaded with defensive Arts.
relics.usage: All allied units have RES +15, and gain one-time dodge after deployment

relics.name: 'Tear of the Departed'
relics.desc: Forged with honesty and perseverance. No flame has burned in Whitesmith's furnace ever since.
relics.usage: All Operators gain 50% Sanctuary when affected by a negative status such as stun, cold or freeze

relics.name: Old Fan
relics.desc: When it rains for several days on end, the old Yanese Tianshi complains to his friends about the pain in his knees, before casually waving his fan and driving away the rainclouds. It is about time he taught this trick to his young apprentice, currently working at the Central Judicial Office.
relics.usage: All Operators gain ATK +10% for every different type of Operator class in the squad

relics.name: Nachzehrer's Cane
relics.desc: Nachzehrer devours life and uses it.
relics.usage: When an allied unit is overhealed, nearby enemies take Arts damage equal to the overheal amount

relics.name: Angelina's Inspiration
relics.desc: The girl has no qualms about sharing her Originium Arts. She believes that you will use it for the right reasons.
relics.usage: All enemy units have Movement Speed -15%, and their weight is reduced by one rank

relics.name: Scout's Scope
relics.desc: Lock on, aim, fire, and destroy the enemy's will to resist, all in the name of a better future that Scout will never see. Maybe you can do it for him.
relics.usage: Enemies take increased damage from allied units as range increases (max 100%)

relics.name: 'Silence'
relics.desc: When the climax arrives, the actor grabs his stinging throat and holds back the roiling fire in his chest, all without a word.

relics.name: 'Focus'

relics.name: Fortification Contract - Phalanx
relics.desc: Like a huge ship running over rubble.

relics.name: Guard Cap

relics.usage: All enemy units have -15% HP and Movement Speed

relics.name: Castle's Offspring
relics.desc: With enough time, this box will become a whole new stage.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +300 DEF and +30 RES 100 seconds after deployment

relics.name: Surging Feast
relics.desc: We've known how to indulge since birth. Do not resist your desires. Submit to them instead.

relics.name: 'Eventide Terror'
relics.desc: There will come a day of darkness that not even light can save. The leader of the troupe has prepared a fine wine to properly appreciate human despair.

variations.name: Enraged
variations.func: On this floor, Combat Ops. will appear more frequently.
variations.desc: They are all enemies, and you thirst to rip and tear your enemies apart!

variations.name: Solitary

endings.name: Crimson Finale
endings.desc: The curtain falls, and the actors all step off the stage. You have found those who you were looking for, and your exploration of the castle has come to an end. Perhaps it is time to return to a normal life.
cond: Prerequisite: none (default ending)
Clear floors 1–5 without holding 「Abrupt Realization」, 「Act 1」, or 「Worn-out Puppet」.
Achieved by defeating the boss Lucian, the 'Blood Diamond', at the floor 5 Dreadful Foe ISW-DF 「The Point of No Return」 (any difficulty).

endings.name: Ridiculous Comedy
endings.desc: The curtain falls, and the actors all step off the stage. You have found those who you were looking for, but no one can say for sure that your exploration of the castle has come to an end. Perhaps this is only the beginning...
endings.change: In a trance, you see a large Ursus doll. Bound by layers of chains, the doll is crying.
cond: Prerequisite: complete Ending 1 「Crimson Finale」 on Formal Investigation difficulty or higher.

Step 1 — at the encounter 「Reprieve?」 (floors 1–4, before the Castle's Bounty at the end of floor 4), choose 'Pick up the doll' → obtain 「Worn-out Puppet」. From this point the floor 5 boss battle becomes ISW-DF 「All I Ask of You」.

Step 2 (optional) — at the encounter 「Crazy Puppets」 (floors 3–5, appears while holding the broken doll), pick 'Hug the doll tight' 3 times (costs 3 Objective HP), then 'Accept' → obtain 「Useless Scissors」.

Step 3 (optional) — at the encounter 「Reprieve」 (floors 4–5, appears while holding the useless scissors), choose 'Ask their name' → obtain 「Blank Suicide Note」. The final boss 「Big Sad Lock」 loses its natural healing.
Achieved by defeating the Weeping Lock at ISW-DF 「All I Ask of You」 while holding neither 「Abrupt Realization」 nor 「Act 1」 (any difficulty).

endings.name: Grand Opening
endings.desc: The host raises the curtain! He bows to the audience alongside his actors as all kinds of tragedies and comedies begin to unfold. He is the one who sets the stage, and he is the one who brings the show to an end. Be sure to offer him flowers when the show is over.
endings.change: You find that the troupe member from Leithanien is behind everything happening in the castle.
cond: Prerequisite: complete Ending 2 「Ridiculous Comedy」 on Formal Investigation difficulty or higher.
Step 1 — at the encounter 「Attending a Banquet」 (floors 1–3, before the Dreadful Foe at the end of floor 3), take the free costume → obtain 「Mantle of the Wrongly Condemned」.
Step 2 — clear the floor 3 Dreadful Foe while holding the tie → pick 「Stand-in Actor」 from the extra clear rewards.
Step 3 — at the encounter 「A Gathering of Followers」 (floor 5, appears while holding the understudy), choose 'Open the door and enter' → obtain 「Abrupt Realization」.
Clear floor 5 while holding 「Abrupt Realization」 → enter the hidden floor 6 「Hall of Desires」 (trader → Safe House → battle → Dreadful Foe).
Achieved by defeating the 'Troupe Spokesperson' at ISW-DF 「Twisted Every Way」 at the end of floor 6 (any difficulty).

endings.name: Silent Chapter
endings.desc: Every word written by the Playwright is true. The truth is blood, is tears, is life, is death, but the truth of the Crimson Troupe goes even deeper. Seek it, discover it, and escape it.
endings.change: 'Ye who has ink shall write with the Playwright a story of the many.'
cond: Prerequisite: complete any ending once on Formal Investigation difficulty or higher (after Expansion II).

Step 1 — at the encounter 「Don't disturb them」 (floors 2–3, before the Dreadful Foe at the end of floor 3), choose 'Sneak closer and listen in' → triggers the special battle 「Bound by Self」; win to obtain 「Dance of the Condemned」.

Step 2 — at the encounter 「Revenge Story」 (floors 4–5, appears while holding the prisoner's dance), choose 'See what they are writing' → triggers the special battle 「Observation」; win to obtain 「Act 1」.
Clear floor 5 while holding 「Act 1」 → enter the hidden floor 6 「Hall of Desires」.
Achieved by defeating the 'Playwright' at ISW-DF 「New Chapter」 at the end of floor 6 (any difficulty).
* Ending priority when holding both 「Abrupt Realization」 and 「Act 1」 has not been confirmed.

encounters.title: Theatrical Necessities
encounters.desc: You find a middle-aged man rambling away in front of a dressing table. He notices you and howls in your direction, asking you to deliver the prop on the table to the actress. You look at the dressing table and find a crown entwined with thorns.

choices.title: Listen to his demands
choices.desc: Lose 1 Life Point, but randomly receive a mysterious collectible

next.desc: You ignore the middle-aged man's howlings and leave.

encounters.title: Dancing Mad
encounters.desc: You happen upon a Sarkaz lady wearing a mask and red dance shoes. She dances alone in the open space, and, noticing you, she reaches her hand out, inviting you to dance with her.

choices.title: Accept the invitation
choices.desc: Lose 3 Originium Ingots, but randomly receive a mysterious collectible

next.desc: Noticing your refusal, the lady ignores you and returns to dancing alone.

encounters.title: Musical Challenge
encounters.desc: You hear a beautiful melody. A wandering jazz player claims to be seeking a legend in the history of art. He traced the troupe's footsteps and made his way here in hopes of joining them. If you would like, he could write a song for you right now. Of course, his troubles demand a small tip.

choices.title: Another one!
choices.desc: Lose 3 Originium Ingots, with a 20% chance to gain 'that thing.'

choices.title: Inspiration, huh... Let's give it a shot
variants: Lose 3 Originium Ingots, with a 40% chance to gain 'that thing.'
Lose 3 Originium Ingots, with a 60% chance to gain 'that thing.'
Lose 3 Originium Ingots, with a 80% chance to gain 'that thing.'
Lose 3 Originium Ingots, with a 100% chance to gain 'that thing.'

encounters.title: 'The Sword in the Stone'
encounters.desc: You stumble on a knight wearing a worn-out set of armor and gloomily looking at a sword in a stone. He claims to be a knight journeying in search of the holy sword, and he must bring this sword back to his kingdom to become its rightful ruler. However, he has no way to pull out the sword from the stone.

choices.title: Might as well smash the sword into two halves
Could use the stone as a hammer
Doesn't look like it's necessary
choices.desc: Is there a definitive link between the sword and the throne?

next.desc:
'Strange stones lying in forts distributing swords is no basis for a system of government. Supreme executive power derives from a mandate from the masses, not from some farcical geologic ceremony.'
The knight thinks you have a point and leaves on a satisfied note.

encounters.title: Trapped Man
encounters.desc: A youthful looking Feline man is hiding under a long table for some reason. Whenever someone comes close, he starts murmuring in an almost frightening way. He insists on staying under the table, but after much work on your part, he believes you are not a ghost here to kidnap him for performances and songs. He is even willing to trade his only treasure with you.

choices.title: Gotta make it a fair trade
choices.desc: Lose all Originium Ingots, but receive a collectible 「Familiar Sculpture」

choices.title: Come out first. We'll talk
choices.desc: Can't rely on someone like this

next.desc: What treasures could he have anyway? Okay, okay. As long as you get out from under the table!

encounters.title: Eccentric Hunchback
encounters.desc: 'I know who it is that you seek, outsider,' says a slender, stooped woman with a strange laugh. 'You don't understand that Feline, do you? You seem to trust him, though.' The strange woman looks at you through her helmet and scarf. 'I can tell you all about his past. Are you interested?' She reaches out her shriveled hands, seemingly asking for something.

next.desc: 'As a member of the troupe, he was very well trained. 'Shadow' taught him illusion Arts. 'Bladedance' taught him his dance-like combat techniques. 'Whiteflower' taught him his exquisite acting skills. As for His Lordship, he gave him his charming singing voice... When the Feline finally learned the truth and realized his respected teachers were all hopeless villains... What a long, bloody night it was...'
'Ah, 'Shadow.' A most delicate but cruel murderer. He was a villain who lurked in the shadows. Alas, his student evidently knew how to blend into the darkness even better than he.'
'Ah, the beautiful dancer. Sharp as a blade, and cold as a blizzard. Too bad, then, that the young Feline was swifter. I thought they were on very good terms. How regrettable.'

'Oh, 'Whiteflower.' Hahaha. The woman was a fine actor, but her performance could never hide what a vicious, despicable person she was. Dying at the hands of her own student was a fate too kind. Hahahaha.'
'How could be so brave as to ask about him? What a reckless lunatic. For your fearlessness, I will give you your money back, but I don't want to die yet. I can't tell you anything.'

choices.title: Guess not, then
choices.desc: The Originium Ingot wasn't carried here by the dust

next.desc: This is too sketchy. Forget it.

encounters.title: Sleeping Statue
encounters.desc: By mistake, you barge into a large, empty hall. The hall is packed with all kinds of stone statues, dressed in different outfits. The statues stand in different poses, and their details are eerily lifelike. You notice that all of the statues have their eyes closed as though sleeping.

next.desc: 'Awaken! Rouse from your slumber!' The chance to say things like this doesn't come by often, and even though there may be a dear price to pay, there will always be some who thinks it's worth it.
Let's not disturb the sleeping statues, especially when they have gathered here in such large numbers. You heed the advice you received before and quietly head for the door.

encounters.title: Shackled Bloodline
encounters.desc: In a dark jail, you find a slender girl shackled to a stone column by multiple chains. Noticing you, the girl claims to be a member of the Sarkaz Vampire royal family. She asks you to give her a little blood to restore her strength and break free from her restraints.

next.desc: After a loud roar, the girl rips the thick chains apart and laugh manically. She loudly ridicules the 'Troupe' who locked her here and swears to take revenge against every last one of them. Her fine facial features suddenly crumble into three segments, revealing her sharp, horrifying fangs. Subsequently, the shadows in the jail morph into several human-like shapes, and the twisted beings all attack you at once.

choices.title: Don't look for trouble!
choices.desc: There has to be a reason this person's all chained up

next.desc: Seeing you walk away indifferently, the shackled girl lets out a terrifying scream. You are thankful that you didn't stick your hand where it doesn't belong.

encounters.title: Phantom Ramblings
encounters.desc: As you explore the long corridor, an eerie voice suddenly speaks to you: 'Your actions in Kazimierz have piqued my interest, which is why I prepared a wonderful encounter for you right here. Would you be so kind to take part in a knight duel performance with my actors?' Not a second after the voice finishes, four knights step out of the shadows. Though they look familiar, you can feel that they are not anyone you are acquainted with.

choices.title: Accept the invitation
choices.desc: Let's see what the hell you're trying to pull

next.desc: 'It matters not who reigns victorious in the end. Your participation has given our dull program an uplifting improvement. You have my thanks, dear traveler from afar.'
Noticing that you are not responding to his request, the voice lets out a sigh, and the four knights fall back into the shadows, never to be seen again.

encounters.title: Art and Madness
encounters.desc: 'They're nuts! They're all nuts! Help!' You hear a loud plea for help through the door. At the same time, you notice a sweet, strange aroma wafting in the air.

choices.title: Go in for a look
choices.desc: Maybe there's something in it for me?

next.desc: You open the door and find yourself immediately bombarded by the strong aroma. A large group of well-dressed artists are engaged in a bloodbath inside the house, all of them delirious. There are already many bodies lying dead on the floor. Soon, they will be attacking you.
It's better not to look for trouble. Let's walk away.

encounters.title: Coffin of Evil
encounters.desc: You discover a dark room deep inside the castle. There are no windows in the room, nor are there any light sources. In the center of the room is a massive coffin with many gems and jewels affixed. Just as you are about to inspect the coffin, you hear a voice from inside it. It seems to be asking for your help to open the coffin.

choices.title: We can't stand idly by when someone's asking for help
choices.desc: Lose 3 Life Points, but receive a collectible 「Proof of Friendship」

encounters.title: Eye For an Eye
encounters.desc: A refined, well-dressed Sarkaz gentleman stands before you. He politely asks if you know of an innocent man who was once wrongfully punished because of him. You ask him to elaborate and learn that, because of a mistake he made, the villagers all mistook his friend for a Vampire and locked him here. The man promises a sizable reward if you lend him your help.

choices.title: Lead the way
choices.desc: Lose 2 Life Points, but gain 3 Hope

encounters.title: Secret Entrance
encounters.desc: In your confusion, you smash through a wall to find a large, empty space hidden behind it. The room's furnishings highlight its user's unique artistic sensibilities. There are all kinds of antiques you have never seen plastered all over the wall. In the center of the room, an inscribed tablet brimming with historical richness, attracts your attention.

encounters.title: Secret Chamber
encounters.desc: You trip over your exhausted feet and tumble down the castle's staircase, only to find a hidden trap door. It leads to a bedchamber with exquisite furnishings not seen anywhere else in the castle. The bedchamber is spotless as though it is cleaned on a regular basis. You notice there is a delicately carved sculpture on the work desk.

encounters.title: Junkyard Bazaar
encounters.desc: In an open area of the castle, you see an unkempt Feline hawking goods. He notices you and enthusiastically promotes treasures that were 'collected from all around the castle through countless trials and tribulations.' However, you seem to think everything he is selling is useless junk.

choices.title: Buy something
choices.desc: Lose 6 Originium Ingot, but receive a random collectible

choices.title: I don't buy junk
choices.desc: The Originium Ingot wasn't carried here by the dust

next.desc: Who would do business with a shady guy like this?

encounters.title: 'Art Connoisseur'
encounters.desc:
'You have several pieces of art on you that I believe belonged to a famous artist. Would you be so kind as to let me examine them?'
You meet a well-dressed, polite woman deep inside the castle. She seems to be interested in some of the artwork you have come across.

choices.title: Show her both
choices.desc: Receive Collectible 「'Beauty and Ugliness in the Age of Terra'」

next.desc: 'That is unfortunate, then.' You notice the art connoisseur's rigid smile is hiding a hint of hostility.

encounters.title: A Tiny Stage
encounters.desc: The tiny stage in the corner of the hall attracts your attention. You walk closer to find that there is already a knight on the stage. The performance is almost ready to begin, pending just one more character gracing the stage. Where is the character? You look at the coin slot to the side.

choices.title: Sounds too good to be true.
A shame...
choices.desc: War and profit don't always go hand in hand

next.desc: The curtain falls, and the characters all leave the stage one by one, but after a period of silence, the curtain rises again with the lone knight on the stage, just as before.

choices.title: Thanks for the Originium, kind knight
Let's not waste any more time

next.desc: The plays performed on the stage are all the same. You have lost interest in them, but you are suddenly reminded of some historical records you once read. The crude stage play being performed before your eyes seems very similar to a certain historical event.

choices.title: Is there anything left to be happy about?

encounters.title: Curio Keeper
encounters.desc: You come across an old lady sitting on top of a tall pile of boxes, reading fairy tales. She notices you, and beckons you toward her. According to her, all these were once treasures, but their owner lost everything he ever had and was forgotten by history, leaving not so much as a gravestone. Thus, all the curios became 'junk' without owners. If you're willing to spend some coins and listen to the old lady tell the tale of this box's owner, you will become its new owner.

choices.title: I'm up for a story
choices.desc: Lose 5 Originium Ingots, but has a chance to receive a collectible

next.desc: You ask the old lady where she heard all these stories. She thinks to herself and, with a smile, she tells you she has a Caprinae granddaughter, and she heard all the stories from her granddaughter. The old lady is a Feline. Subconsciously, you think she may be hiding something, but the truth is you find the story decent, and what's inside the box might come in handy. After some small talk, the old lady asks if you would like to hear another story.

choices.title: Pass, I don't think it's worth it

next.desc: The old lady sees that you are not very interested in listening to any more stories and returns to her book without saying more. As you leave, you notice the letters on the book's cover written in Victorian: Geoffrey's New Fairy Tales

choices.title: Is there anything left to be happy about?

encounters.title: Potent Potables
encounters.desc: At a wine cellar, you come across a few Durins in a drinking contest. The castle's centuries-old collection of fine wine is being squandered lavishly, yet they claim they have the permission of the collection's owner. Seeing that you are here, they roll a barrel of fine wine toward you and invite you to join in on the fun.

choices.title: Down a third of the barrel
choices.desc: Lose 2 Life Points, but gain 6 Originium Ingots

choices.title: Down half the barrel
choices.desc: Lose 3 Life Points, but gain a random Recruitment Voucher

choices.title: Down the whole barrel
choices.desc: Lose 6 Life Points, but receive a random collectible

choices.title: Down even more
choices.desc: Won't have many more chances to sample such a fine liquor again

next.desc: The Durins will stay until the cellar is completely devoid of liquor, but it is time for you to take your leave. You still have other things to attend to.

choices.title: Is there anything left to be happy about?

encounters.title: The Rat Thieves
encounters.desc: You wander aimlessly in the castle. By accident, you step on a rotten patch of the flooring and fall into the castle's basement. Here, you find a heap of garbage as well as three doleful-looking Zalaks. The Zalaks confess that they are treasure hunters who have painstakingly searched for the castle's treasure chamber, only to find that someone has been here before them. The chamber has nothing but a pile of trash left.

next.desc: Let's not waste any more time here.
Let's not waste any more time here.

choices.title: Is there anything left to be happy about?
choices.desc: Obtain all spent Hope and Max Hope

encounters.title: Crimson Masquerade
encounters.desc: A truly bustling masquerade. There are countless dancers wearing full dresses and masks around. They waltz past you as the accompanying pieces of enthusiastic music echo throughout the hall one after another. None of them feel the slightest bit of exhaustion even when their voices go hoarse. None of them are stopping even when their shoes are completely worn out and their feet bleeding. You can't see their faces as all of them are wearing masks that feign happiness.

choices.title: Wear an extravagant outfit
choices.desc: Lose 10 Originium Ingots, but gain 8 Life Points

choices.title: Offer flowers to the dancer
choices.desc: Lose 10 Originium Ingots, but gain 6 Hope

choices.title: Dance with her
choices.desc: Lose 4 Max Hope and Hope, but gain 10 Life Points.

choices.title: Sing a song
choices.desc: Lose 5 Life Points, but gain 8 Hope

choices.title: Take the dancer's mask off
choices.desc: Lose 5 Life Points, but gain 15 Originium Ingots

encounters.title: The Kind Puppet
encounters.desc: A worn-out wooden puppet sits next to the wall, its limbs almost falling apart. Its hazy facial features seem to ring a bell, but you cannot say for certain who it is given how worn-out it is. You come close to the puppet and find that there seems to be something hidden inside its chest.
encounters.note: Can appear more than once in the same run

encounters.title: Attending a Banquet
encounters.desc: A group of Leithanian nobles is hurriedly making its way to a party. One of them tells you that the organizer of the party is a spire caster obsessed with the performing arts, favoring grandiose functions and extravagance. The man has very high standards for all of his guests. The Leithanian noble takes a look at you and proposes the following after a brief moment of pondering: If you will attend the party, he is willing to transfer the invitation to you and provide an outfit as proper as his for free.

choices.title: Get a free outfit
choices.desc: Receive Collectible 「Mantle of the Wrongly Condemned」

choices.title: Cherish your life, stay away from social events!

encounters.note: Starts the branch for Ending 3 「Grand Opening」 — take the free costume to obtain 「Mantle of the Wrongly Condemned」 (can be met until the Dreadful Foe at the end of floor 3)

encounters.title: A Gathering of Followers
encounters.desc: A heated conversation can be heard from inside the room. You listen carefully and make out bits and pieces through the thick Leithanian accent. 'He killed everyone at the party? Just for that play of his?' 'Didn't I say from the start we should never have let Ahrendts know about our plans. He's been out of his mind ever since he ran into that Crimson Troupe!' 'Don't say that, Andreas! At least he's actually done some research on Arts. Besides, I think those artists aren't so bad...' 'Hah, looks like you're pretty far gone yourself!'

choices.title: Swing the doors open and walk in

next.desc: There seems to be quite a few people inside. It would be best to come back after you have made the necessary preparations.

encounters.note: Appears only while holding 「Stand-in Actor」 — choose 'Open the door and enter' to obtain 「Abrupt Realization」 (the key to the hidden floor)

encounters.title: Camp
encounters.desc: You find a quiet corner safe enough to harbor your entire squad from harm, allowing you to rest and reorganize. Having helped your teammates settle in, your consciousness slowly begins to loosen up.
encounters.note: Can appear more than once in the same run

encounters.title: Teller of Tales
encounters.desc: You sit next to a simple campfire at the castle's terrace, trying to get some rest. Next to you sits a special passerby. The talkative adventurer has shared with you many over-the-top stories: 'I heard the Columbian president is actually a machine...' 'I've been told that there are some slowly moving mountain ranges in the Fjal Vetrtonn of Sami...' 'Some people say there are some twisted experiments being run behind the scenes of the endless war in Bolívar. Those scary things they call Hounds were actually created in those experiments...'
encounters.note: Can appear more than once in the same run

encounters.title: Adventurer Commission
encounters.desc: You come across a wandering adventurer. He tells you about some strange happenings he came upon lately. Supposedly, there have is some grandiose supernatural playhouse with phantoms partying hard on the stage each and every night. His search has proven to be futile, and he was almost attacked by a group of madmen spouting nonsense. He is preparing to leave now, and perhaps we can entrust to him a request for help from our allies waiting outside.

choices.title: Give a small gift
choices.desc: Lose 2 Originium Ingots, but recruit one Operator (from a selection of 2 random classes)

choices.title: Give a big reward
choices.desc: Lose 5 Originium Ingots, but gain a Recruitment Voucher

choices.title: Ask sincerely
choices.desc: Trust that sincerity will move him

next.desc: The adventurer gives you his word, but no one comes to your aid, no matter how long you wait.

encounters.title: 'Royal Rumble'
encounters.desc: In a strange, frivolously decorated room, there are two extravagantly dressed Liberi, both with crowns on their head, locked in a fiery standoff. They claim to be the sixth and eighth emperor of Gaul, and they are debating the achievements and missteps of Corsica I, the last emperor of Gaul. Their debate is getting out of hand, and things are about to come to blows.

choices.title: Denounce Corsica I
choices.desc: Recruit one Operator (from a selection of 2 random classes)

choices.title: Sing the praises of Corsica I
choices.desc: Lose 8 Originium Ingots, but gain a Recruitment Voucher

encounters.title: Safe House
encounters.desc: There is danger lying in wait in every corner of the old castle. The unusual environment, the enemies lurking in the shadows, and the grotesque happenings have been unceasing, wearing you and your squad out. Fortunately, you are able to find a corner to catch a breather every now and then. You would do well to manage your resources, relax, and plan your next steps quickly. Your next challenge will appear before long.
encounters.note: One is always placed on the hidden floor 6 (floors 1–5 depend on node layout)

encounters.title: Reprieve?
encounters.desc: You reach a hall with an oil painting in a most conspicuous location. The painting depicts the ending of a tragic story. At the end of the story, the protagonist discovers that his entire life is a fabricated conspiracy. In his grief, he leaps off a tall tower as a last-ditch effort to break free of their control.

choices.title: Poor guy. Let's take a closer look
Better stay away from tragic stories!

encounters.note: Starts the branch for Ending 2 「Ridiculous Comedy」 — picking 'Pick up the doll' grants 「Worn-out Puppet」 and swaps the floor 5 boss (can be met until the Castle's Bounty node at the end of floor 4)

encounters.title: Crazy Puppets
encounters.desc: A strange voice calls out from down the hallway. A swarm of puppets walking in a strange rhythm block your way. The puppets charge toward you, but it seems you are not their target. It is the broken puppet.

next.desc: The puppets continue their advance. There is no way to break through, leaving you with no choice but to leave.

encounters.note: Appears only while holding 「Worn-out Puppet」 — pick 'Hug the doll tight' 3 times (costs 3 Objective HP), then 'Accept' to obtain 「Useless Scissors」

encounters.title: Reprieve
encounters.desc: You walk up the stairs and notice a familiar figure standing next to the window. The figure suddenly turns around and thanks you, before squeezing a letter into your hand.
encounters.note: Appears only while holding 「Useless Scissors」 — choose 'Ask their name' to obtain 「Blank Suicide Note」 (disables the final boss's natural healing)

encounters.title: Duck Lord the Lead Actor?
encounters.desc: The curtain rises before you, and you are the only members of the audience today. Tonight's performance is a renowned Gaulish tragicomedy. Its talented female lead even danced till her shoes were soaked with blood during a curtain call one time as the audience cheered her on—Hey, what the duck? Why is there's a duck scattering cash around the stage?

choices.title: Get the lead actress out here!

next.desc: Infuriated, you stomp onto the stage, hell bent on getting this duck off the stage!
Surprisingly, the duck's steps aren't too bad.

encounters.title: Duck Lord's Party
encounters.desc: The duck, apparently called the Duck Lord, invites you to a party. The entire table is packed full of the finest beverages and most decadent desserts Gaul has to offer. The other guests sitting at the table are also chatting away about various artistic subjects. Suddenly, the duck snaps his fingers, and everyone falls silent. 'I know why all of you are here, friends,' the duck says. 'I suggest you all go home while you still have the chance.'

choices.title: The hell should I listen to you for?

next.desc: Naturally, you aren't about to listen to a duck's quackings, but it seems he has some inside information—You decide to capture him!

choices.title: He's right. The duck has a point

next.desc: You persuade the artists gathered here that this isn't a nice place to be.

encounters.title: Duck Lord's Improv
encounters.desc: You come across the duck who calls himself the Duck Lord once again. He is harshly mocking a puppet, which he claims to be an improvised performance. The program's title is 'How Investors Mock Investees.' You take a closer look and notice that a picture of the famous Columbian rapper Emperor is nailed to the puppet.

choices.title: This is supposed to be a play?

next.desc: You are about to argue with him. The stalwart Ursus man behind him nods and raises his fist.
This might just be how the rich live, though you are nonetheless curious about this duck's relation to Emperor.

encounters.title: About That Ursus Man
encounters.desc: In the corner of a rundown storeroom lies a large amount of Ursus military uniforms and weapons. You notice a familiar figure. The stalwart Ursus man who usually follows the Duck Lord around is crouched on the ground with a melancholic look. Every now and then, he takes a peek at the photograph in his hand. You steal a glance and realize it's a photograph of the Ursus man in uniform and a Hippogryph general.

choices.title: Flip over the boxes and crates
choices.desc: There's definitely some good stuff here

next.desc: You ignore the Ursus man and start flipping through the boxes and shelves in the room. Your rude behavior angers him, and he picks up a weapon beside him to attack you.
Everyone goes through ups and downs, and everyone has moments they find difficult to look back on.

encounters.title: Hallucinatory Candlelights
encounters.desc: In the center part of the castle, you find a chapel enveloped by a cloud of haze. There is a sweet, yet strange smell wafting from the chapel. The smell seems to originate from the countless scented candles inside the chapel. You notice what seems to be some treasures sitting on the chapel's lectern.

choices.title: It's just a candle. Nothing to be afraid of
choices.desc: Lose 2 Life Points, but gain 8 Originium Ingots

next.desc: Let's not give ourselves any trouble.

encounters.title: Columbian Brewer
encounters.desc: You see a group of adventurers and treasure hunters having a good time over beer. Next to them are a few Columbians selling large amounts of beer. You talk to them and learn that they work for a small Columbian brewery whose delivery truck was damaged in a Catastrophe, and they opted to sell their wares to any passing visitors.

choices.title: A few bottles for me too?
choices.desc: Lose 5 Originium Ingots, but gain 3 Hope

choices.title: Still got stuff to do. I'll pass

next.desc: Now isn't the time to drink.

encounters.title: 'Tin Man'
encounters.desc: You come across a strange man wearing a trench coat. His face, shadowed by the large fedora he is wearing, is composed of metallic, mechanical parts. He claims to be a detective hired by the 'Maylander Historical Association' to investigate the mysterious organization, 'Crimson Troupe.' However, like you, he is trapped here now. You realize you might be able to share information with each other.

choices.title: Try to share information
choices.desc: Lose 2 Max Hope and Hope, but gain a random collectible.

choices.title: Guess not, then
choices.desc: Once word gets out, the value goes down

next.desc: 'I wish you the best of luck.' The tin detective bids his farewell and disappears down the hallway. Speaking of which, what race was he anyway?

encounters.title: Gift of History
encounters.desc: An old city once perished in a tragedy, and the tragic tale of the citizens' tribulations has been weaved into a play. While their story continues to be told, the treasures of the city have laid dormant until now. You come across some treasures next to the rotten corpse of the story's protagonist. The most precious manuscript, penned by the star herself, is half rotted and lost among the dust.
encounters.note: Castle's Bounty node — appears at the end of floors 2 and 4

encounters.title: Gift of Fate
encounters.desc: You cannot say for certain whether this is reality or merely a dream, but there is a tall stone pedestal with an expensive, old-fashioned looking box on top. Perhaps someone left it here, or perhaps it has been waiting here for you. Regardless, it is a gift.

choices.title: Receive gift
choices.desc: Receive a random collectible which might turn out to be a special plaything

choices.title: Pick up the treasures underneath the stone pedestal

encounters.note: Castle's Bounty node — appears at the end of floors 2 and 4, with a guaranteed rare collectible option

encounters.title: Advance Squad
encounters.desc: You locate the Rhodes Island squad who sent out the original distress signal. They have been scouting the surrounding areas before you made contact. You exchange information with each other, and their squad shares some of their resources and experience with you.

encounters.title: Gaulish Veteran
encounters.desc: You happen upon an old, veteran soldier in tattered clothing who's sitting on top of the castle's ruins. You chat with him a little and learn about his past. The man claims to be a Gaulish Vanguard who once fought alongside the emperor. He is here to seek the emperor's treasures to contribute to Gaul's revival. As your conversation continues, the man starts an impassioned speech, impressing upon you his homeland's past glory.

encounters.title: Don't disturb them
encounters.desc: You barge into a dungeon. The prisoners within are silent, showing no reaction to your sudden entry. You see some of them carving on the walls, while others curl up on the ground, muttering to themselves.

choices.title: Get closer and eavesdrop
choices.desc: Maybe we can hear something useful

next.desc: 'Why do you disturb me? Why do you not let me finish my story?!' One of the prisoners suddenly stands up and rams the cell door.
You leave the dungeon. As you walk away, you hear the sound of exultant laughter coming from behind.

encounters.note: Starts the branch for Ending 4 「Silent Chapter」 — choose 'Sneak closer and listen in', then win the special battle 「Bound by Self」 to obtain 「Dance of the Condemned」 (available after floor 1 and before the end of floor 3)

encounters.title: Revenge Story
encounters.desc: In the dimly-lit room, you see a young man sat by himself before a desk, dipping his quill into a bottle of strangely-colored ink and writing intently in the flickering candlelight. He does not notice your approach.

next.desc: You approach the young man. His quill suddenly begins to burn, and he disappears in the rising flames and smoke. You find yourself surrounded by a bunch of lunatics.
The young man stops, places his quill into the inkwell, and leaves.

encounters.note: Appears only while holding 「Dance of the Condemned」 — choose 'See what they are writing', then win the special battle 「Observation」 to obtain 「Act 1」
